symbols.name: push
symbols.description:
Copies and pushes the current coordinate transformation to the transformation stack.

This function is always used to prepare for a corresponding pop operation later. It stores the current coordinate transformation state into the transformation stack and keeps it active. Later changes to the transformation can be undone by using the pop operation, which returns the coordinate transform to the state it was in before calling push.

arguments.name: stack
arguments.description: The type of stack to push (e.g. just transformation state, or all love.graphics state).

symbols.name: rectangle
symbols.description: Draws a rectangle.

arguments.name: mode
arguments.description: How to draw the rectangle.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: The position of top-left corner along the x-axis.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: The position of top-left corner along the y-axis.

arguments.name: width
arguments.description: Width of the rectangle.

arguments.name: height
arguments.description: Height of the rectangle.

arguments.name: mode
arguments.description: How to draw the rectangle.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: The position of top-left corner along the x-axis.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: The position of top-left corner along the y-axis.

arguments.name: width
arguments.description: Width of the rectangle.

arguments.name: height
arguments.description: Height of the rectangle.

arguments.name: rx
arguments.description: The x-axis radius of each round corner. Cannot be greater than half the rectangle's width.

arguments.name: ry
arguments.description: The y-axis radius of each round corner. Cannot be greater than half the rectangle's height.

arguments.name: segments
arguments.description: The number of segments used for drawing the round corners. A default amount will be chosen if no number is given.

symbols.name: replaceTransform
symbols.description: Replaces the current coordinate transformation with the given Transform object.

arguments.name: transform
arguments.description: The Transform object to replace the current graphics coordinate transform with.

symbols.name: reset
symbols.description:
Resets the current graphics settings.

Calling reset makes the current drawing color white, the current background color black, disables any active color component masks, disables wireframe mode and resets the current graphics transformation to the origin. It also sets both the point and line drawing modes to smooth and their sizes to 1.0.

symbols.name: rotate
symbols.description:
Rotates the coordinate system in two dimensions.

Calling this function affects all future drawing operations by rotating the coordinate system around the origin by the given amount of radians. This change lasts until love.draw() exits.

arguments.name: angle
arguments.description: The amount to rotate the coordinate system in radians.

symbols.name: scale
symbols.description:
Scales the coordinate system in two dimensions.

By default the coordinate system in LÖVE corresponds to the display pixels in horizontal and vertical directions one-to-one, and the x-axis increases towards the right while the y-axis increases downwards. Scaling the coordinate system changes this relation.

After scaling by sx and sy, all coordinates are treated as if they were multiplied by sx and sy. Every result of a drawing operation is also correspondingly scaled, so scaling by (2, 2) for example would mean making everything twice as large in both x- and y-directions. Scaling by a negative value flips the coordinate system in the corresponding direction, which also means everything will be drawn flipped or upside down, or both. Scaling by zero is not a useful operation.

Scale and translate are not commutative operations, therefore, calling them in different orders will change the outcome.

Scaling lasts until love.draw() exits.

arguments.name: sx
arguments.description: The scaling in the direction of the x-axis.

arguments.name: sy
arguments.description: The scaling in the direction of the y-axis. If omitted, it defaults to same as parameter sx.

symbols.name: setBackgroundColor
symbols.description: Sets the background color.

arguments.name: red
arguments.description: The red component (0-1).

arguments.name: green
arguments.description: The green component (0-1).

arguments.name: blue
arguments.description: The blue component (0-1).

arguments.name: alpha
arguments.description: The alpha component (0-1).

arguments.name: rgba
arguments.description: A numerical indexed table with the red, green, blue and alpha values as numbers. The alpha is optional and defaults to 1 if it is left out.

symbols.name: setBlendMode
symbols.description: Sets the blending mode.

arguments.name: mode
arguments.description: The blend mode to use.

arguments.name: mode
arguments.description: The blend mode to use.

arguments.name: alphamode
arguments.description: What to do with the alpha of drawn objects when blending.

symbols.name: setCanvas
symbols.description: Captures drawing operations to a Canvas.

arguments.name: canvas
arguments.description: The new target.

arguments.name: mipmap
arguments.description: The mipmap level to render to, for Canvases with mipmaps.

arguments.name: canvas1
arguments.description: The first render target.

arguments.name: canvas2
arguments.description: The second render target.
More canvases.

arguments.name: canvas
arguments.description: The new render target.

arguments.name: slice
arguments.description: For cubemaps this is the cube face index to render to (between 1 and 6). For Array textures this is the array layer. For volume textures this is the depth slice. 2D canvases should use a value of 1.

arguments.name: mipmap
arguments.description: The mipmap level to render to, for Canvases with mipmaps.

arguments.name: setup
arguments.description: A table specifying the active Canvas(es), their mipmap levels and active layers if applicable, and whether to use a stencil and/or depth buffer.

symbols.name: setColor
symbols.description:
Sets the color used for drawing.

In versions prior to 11.0, color component values were within the range of 0 to 255 instead of 0 to 1.

arguments.name: red
arguments.description: The amount of red.

arguments.name: green
arguments.description: The amount of green.

arguments.name: blue
arguments.description: The amount of blue.

arguments.name: alpha
arguments.description: The amount of alpha.  The alpha value will be applied to all subsequent draw operations, even the drawing of an image.

arguments.name: rgba
arguments.description: A numerical indexed table with the red, green, blue and alpha values as numbers. The alpha is optional and defaults to 1 if it is left out.

symbols.name: setColorMask
symbols.description: Sets the color mask. Enables or disables specific color components when rendering and clearing the screen. For example, if '''red''' is set to '''false''', no further changes will be made to the red component of any pixels.

arguments.name: red
arguments.description: Render red component.

arguments.name: green
arguments.description: Render green component.

arguments.name: blue
arguments.description: Render blue component.

arguments.name: alpha
arguments.description: Render alpha component.

symbols.name: setDefaultFilter
symbols.description: Sets the default scaling filters used with Images, Canvases, and Fonts.

arguments.name: min
arguments.description: Filter mode used when scaling the image down.

arguments.name: mag
arguments.description: Filter mode used when scaling the image up.

arguments.name: anisotropy
arguments.description: Maximum amount of Anisotropic Filtering used.

symbols.name: setDepthMode
symbols.description:
Configures depth testing and writing to the depth buffer.

This is low-level functionality designed for use with custom vertex shaders and Meshes with custom vertex attributes. No higher level APIs are provided to set the depth of 2D graphics such as shapes, lines, and Images.

arguments.name: comparemode
arguments.description: Depth comparison mode used for depth testing.

arguments.name: write
arguments.description: Whether to write update / write values to the depth buffer when rendering.

symbols.name: setFont
symbols.description:
Set an already-loaded Font as the current font or create and load a new one from the file and size.

It's recommended that Font objects are created with love.graphics.newFont in the loading stage and then passed to this function in the drawing stage.

arguments.name: font
arguments.description: The Font object to use.

symbols.name: setFrontFaceWinding
symbols.description:
Sets whether triangles with clockwise- or counterclockwise-ordered vertices are considered front-facing.

This is designed for use in combination with Mesh face culling. Other love.graphics shapes, lines, and sprites are not guaranteed to have a specific winding order to their internal vertices.

arguments.name: winding
arguments.description: The winding mode to use. The default winding is counterclockwise ('ccw').

symbols.name: setLineJoin
symbols.description: Sets the line join style. See LineJoin for the possible options.

arguments.name: join
arguments.description: The LineJoin to use.

symbols.name: setLineStyle
symbols.description: Sets the line style.

arguments.name: style
arguments.description: The LineStyle to use. Line styles include smooth and rough.

symbols.name: setLineWidth
symbols.description: Sets the line width.

arguments.name: width
arguments.description: The width of the line.

symbols.name: setMeshCullMode
symbols.description:
Sets whether back-facing triangles in a Mesh are culled.

This is designed for use with low level custom hardware-accelerated 3D rendering via custom vertex attributes on Meshes, custom vertex shaders, and depth testing with a depth buffer.

By default, both front- and back-facing triangles in Meshes are rendered.

arguments.name: mode
arguments.description: The Mesh face culling mode to use (whether to render everything, cull back-facing triangles, or cull front-facing triangles).

symbols.name: setNewFont
symbols.description: Creates and sets a new Font.

arguments.name: size
arguments.description: The size of the font.

returns.name: font
returns.description: The new font.

arguments.name: filename
arguments.description: The path and name of the file with the font.

arguments.name: size
arguments.description: The size of the font.

returns.name: font
returns.description: The new font.

arguments.name: file
arguments.description: A File with the font.

arguments.name: size
arguments.description: The size of the font.

returns.name: font
returns.description: The new font.

arguments.name: data
arguments.description: A Data with the font.

arguments.name: size
arguments.description: The size of the font.

returns.name: font
returns.description: The new font.

arguments.name: rasterizer
arguments.description: A rasterizer.

returns.name: font
returns.description: The new font.

symbols.name: setPointSize
symbols.description: Sets the point size.

arguments.name: size
arguments.description: The new point size.

symbols.name: setScissor
symbols.description:
Sets or disables scissor.

The scissor limits the drawing area to a specified rectangle. This affects all graphics calls, including love.graphics.clear.

The dimensions of the scissor is unaffected by graphical transformations (translate, scale, ...).

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: x coordinate of upper left corner.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: y coordinate of upper left corner.

arguments.name: width
arguments.description: width of clipping rectangle.

arguments.name: height
arguments.description: height of clipping rectangle.

symbols.name: setShader
symbols.description: Sets or resets a Shader as the current pixel effect or vertex shaders. All drawing operations until the next ''love.graphics.setShader'' will be drawn using the Shader object specified.

arguments.name: shader
arguments.description: The new shader.

symbols.name: setStencilTest
symbols.description:
Configures or disables stencil testing.

When stencil testing is enabled, the geometry of everything that is drawn afterward will be clipped / stencilled out based on a comparison between the arguments of this function and the stencil value of each pixel that the geometry touches. The stencil values of pixels are affected via love.graphics.stencil.

arguments.name: comparemode
arguments.description: The type of comparison to make for each pixel.

arguments.name: comparevalue
arguments.description: The value to use when comparing with the stencil value of each pixel. Must be between 0 and 255.

symbols.name: setWireframe
symbols.description: Sets whether wireframe lines will be used when drawing.

arguments.name: enable
arguments.description: True to enable wireframe mode when drawing, false to disable it.

symbols.name: shear
symbols.description: Shears the coordinate system.

arguments.name: kx
arguments.description: The shear factor on the x-axis.

arguments.name: ky
arguments.description: The shear factor on the y-axis.

symbols.name: stencil
symbols.description:
Draws geometry as a stencil.

The geometry drawn by the supplied function sets invisible stencil values of pixels, instead of setting pixel colors. The stencil buffer (which contains those stencil values) can act like a mask / stencil - love.graphics.setStencilTest can be used afterward to determine how further rendering is affected by the stencil values in each pixel.

Stencil values are integers within the range of 255.

arguments.name: stencilfunction
arguments.description: Function which draws geometry. The stencil values of pixels, rather than the color of each pixel, will be affected by the geometry.

arguments.name: action
arguments.description: How to modify any stencil values of pixels that are touched by what's drawn in the stencil function.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: The new stencil value to use for pixels if the 'replace' stencil action is used. Has no effect with other stencil actions. Must be between 0 and 255.

arguments.name: keepvalues
arguments.description: True to preserve old stencil values of pixels, false to re-set every pixel's stencil value to 0 before executing the stencil function. love.graphics.clear will also re-set all stencil values.

symbols.name: transformPoint
symbols.description:
Converts the given 2D position from global coordinates into screen-space.

This effectively applies the current graphics transformations to the given position. A similar Transform:transformPoint method exists for Transform objects.

arguments.name: globalX
arguments.description: The x component of the position in global coordinates.

arguments.name: globalY
arguments.description: The y component of the position in global coordinates.

returns.name: screenX
returns.description: The x component of the position with graphics transformations applied.

returns.name: screenY
returns.description: The y component of the position with graphics transformations applied.

symbols.name: translate
symbols.description:
Translates the coordinate system in two dimensions.

When this function is called with two numbers, dx, and dy, all the following drawing operations take effect as if their x and y coordinates were x+dx and y+dy.

Scale and translate are not commutative operations, therefore, calling them in different orders will change the outcome.

This change lasts until love.draw() exits or else a love.graphics.pop reverts to a previous love.graphics.push.

Translating using whole numbers will prevent tearing/blurring of images and fonts draw after translating.

arguments.name: dx
arguments.description: The translation relative to the x-axis.

arguments.name: dy
arguments.description: The translation relative to the y-axis.

symbols.name: validateShader
symbols.description: Validates shader code. Check if specified shader code does not contain any errors.

arguments.name: gles
arguments.description: Validate code as GLSL ES shader.

arguments.name: code
arguments.description: The pixel shader or vertex shader code, or a filename pointing to a file with the code.

returns.name: status
returns.description: true if specified shader code doesn't contain any errors. false otherwise.

returns.name: message
returns.description: Reason why shader code validation failed (or nil if validation succeded).

arguments.name: gles
arguments.description: Validate code as GLSL ES shader.

arguments.name: pixelcode
arguments.description: The pixel shader code, or a filename pointing to a file with the code.

arguments.name: vertexcode
arguments.description: The vertex shader code, or a filename pointing to a file with the code.

returns.name: status
returns.description: true if specified shader code doesn't contain any errors. false otherwise.

returns.name: message
returns.description: Reason why shader code validation failed (or nil if validation succeded).

symbols.name: generateMipmaps
symbols.description:
Generates mipmaps for the Canvas, based on the contents of the highest-resolution mipmap level.

The Canvas must be created with mipmaps set to a MipmapMode other than 'none' for this function to work. It should only be called while the Canvas is not the active render target.

If the mipmap mode is set to 'auto', this function is automatically called inside love.graphics.setCanvas when switching from this Canvas to another Canvas or to the main screen.

symbols.name: getMSAA
symbols.description:
Gets the number of multisample antialiasing (MSAA) samples used when drawing to the Canvas.

This may be different than the number used as an argument to love.graphics.newCanvas if the system running LÖVE doesn't support that number.

returns.name: samples
returns.description: The number of multisample antialiasing samples used by the canvas when drawing to it.

symbols.name: getMipmapMode
symbols.description: Gets the MipmapMode this Canvas was created with.

returns.name: mode
returns.description: The mipmap mode this Canvas was created with.

symbols.name: newImageData
symbols.description: Generates ImageData from the contents of the Canvas.

returns.name: data
returns.description: The new ImageData made from the Canvas' contents.

arguments.name: slice
arguments.description: The cubemap face index, array index, or depth layer for cubemap, array, or volume type Canvases, respectively. This argument is ignored for regular 2D canvases.

arguments.name: mipmap
arguments.description: The mipmap index to use, for Canvases with mipmaps.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: The x-axis of the top-left corner (in pixels) of the area within the Canvas to capture.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: The y-axis of the top-left corner (in pixels) of the area within the Canvas to capture.

arguments.name: width
arguments.description: The width in pixels of the area within the Canvas to capture.

arguments.name: height
arguments.description: The height in pixels of the area within the Canvas to capture.

returns.name: data
returns.description: The new ImageData made from the Canvas' contents.

symbols.name: renderTo
symbols.description:
Render to the Canvas using a function.

This is a shortcut to love.graphics.setCanvas:

canvas:renderTo( func )

is the same as

love.graphics.setCanvas( canvas )

func()

love.graphics.setCanvas()

arguments.name: func
arguments.description: A function performing drawing operations.
Additional arguments to call the function with.

symbols.name: getAscent
symbols.description:
Gets the ascent of the Font.

The ascent spans the distance between the baseline and the top of the glyph that reaches farthest from the baseline.

returns.name: ascent
returns.description: The ascent of the Font in pixels.

symbols.name: getBaseline
symbols.description:
Gets the baseline of the Font.

Most scripts share the notion of a baseline: an imaginary horizontal line on which characters rest. In some scripts, parts of glyphs lie below the baseline.

returns.name: baseline
returns.description: The baseline of the Font in pixels.

symbols.name: getDPIScale
symbols.description:
Gets the DPI scale factor of the Font.

The DPI scale factor represents relative pixel density. A DPI scale factor of 2 means the font's glyphs have twice the pixel density in each dimension (4 times as many pixels in the same area) compared to a font with a DPI scale factor of 1.

The font size of TrueType fonts is scaled internally by the font's specified DPI scale factor. By default, LÖVE uses the screen's DPI scale factor when creating TrueType fonts.

returns.name: dpiscale
returns.description: The DPI scale factor of the Font.

symbols.name: getDescent
symbols.description:
Gets the descent of the Font.

The descent spans the distance between the baseline and the lowest descending glyph in a typeface.

returns.name: descent
returns.description: The descent of the Font in pixels.

symbols.name: getFilter
symbols.description: Gets the filter mode for a font.

returns.name: min
returns.description: Filter mode used when minifying the font.

returns.name: mag
returns.description: Filter mode used when magnifying the font.

returns.name: anisotropy
returns.description: Maximum amount of anisotropic filtering used.

symbols.name: getHeight
symbols.description:
Gets the height of the Font.

The height of the font is the size including any spacing; the height which it will need.

returns.name: height
returns.description: The height of the Font in pixels.

symbols.name: getKerning
symbols.description:
Gets the kerning between two characters in the Font.

Kerning is normally handled automatically in love.graphics.print, Text objects, Font:getWidth, Font:getWrap, etc. This function is useful when stitching text together manually.

arguments.name: leftchar
arguments.description: The left character.

arguments.name: rightchar
arguments.description: The right character.

returns.name: kerning
returns.description: The kerning amount to add to the spacing between the two characters. May be negative.

arguments.name: leftglyph
arguments.description: The unicode number for the left glyph.

arguments.name: rightglyph
arguments.description: The unicode number for the right glyph.

returns.name: kerning
returns.description: The kerning amount to add to the spacing between the two characters. May be negative.

symbols.name: getLineHeight
symbols.description:
Gets the line height.

This will be the value previously set by Font:setLineHeight, or 1.0 by default.

returns.name: height
returns.description: The current line height.

symbols.name: getWidth
symbols.description: Determines the maximum width (accounting for newlines) taken by the given string.

arguments.name: text
arguments.description: A string.

returns.name: width
returns.description: The width of the text.

symbols.name: getWrap
symbols.description:
Gets formatting information for text, given a wrap limit.

This function accounts for newlines correctly (i.e. '\n').

arguments.name: text
arguments.description: The text that will be wrapped.

arguments.name: wraplimit
arguments.description: The maximum width in pixels of each line that ''text'' is allowed before wrapping.

returns.name: width
returns.description: The maximum width of the wrapped text.

returns.name: wrappedtext
returns.description: A sequence containing each line of text that was wrapped.

arguments.name: coloredtext
arguments.description: A table containing colors and strings to add to the object, in the form of {color1, string1, color2, string2, ...}.

arguments.name: wraplimit
arguments.description: The maximum width in pixels of each line that ''text'' is allowed before wrapping.

returns.name: width
returns.description: The maximum width of the wrapped text.

returns.name: wrappedtext
returns.description: A sequence containing each line of text that was wrapped.

symbols.name: hasGlyphs
symbols.description: Gets whether the Font can render a character or string.

arguments.name: text
arguments.description: A UTF-8 encoded unicode string.

returns.name: hasglyph
returns.description: Whether the font can render all the UTF-8 characters in the string.

arguments.name: character1
arguments.description: A unicode character.

arguments.name: character2
arguments.description: Another unicode character.

returns.name: hasglyph
returns.description: Whether the font can render all the glyphs represented by the characters.

arguments.name: codepoint1
arguments.description: A unicode codepoint number.

arguments.name: codepoint2
arguments.description: Another unicode codepoint number.

returns.name: hasglyph
returns.description: Whether the font can render all the glyphs represented by the codepoint numbers.

symbols.name: setFallbacks
symbols.description: Sets the fallback fonts. When the Font doesn't contain a glyph, it will substitute the glyph from the next subsequent fallback Fonts. This is akin to setting a 'font stack' in Cascading Style Sheets (CSS).

arguments.name: fallbackfont1
arguments.description: The first fallback Font to use.
Additional fallback Fonts.

symbols.name: setFilter
symbols.description: Sets the filter mode for a font.

arguments.name: min
arguments.description: How to scale a font down.

arguments.name: mag
arguments.description: How to scale a font up.

arguments.name: anisotropy
arguments.description: Maximum amount of anisotropic filtering used.

symbols.name: setLineHeight
symbols.description:
Sets the line height.

When rendering the font in lines the actual height will be determined by the line height multiplied by the height of the font. The default is 1.0.

arguments.name: height
arguments.description: The new line height.

symbols.name: isCompressed
symbols.description:
Gets whether the Image was created from CompressedData.

Compressed images take up less space in VRAM, and drawing a compressed image will generally be more efficient than drawing one created from raw pixel data.

returns.name: compressed
returns.description: Whether the Image is stored as a compressed texture on the GPU.

symbols.name: isFormatLinear
symbols.description:
Gets whether the Image was created with the linear (non-gamma corrected) flag set to true.

This method always returns false when gamma-correct rendering is not enabled.

returns.name: linear
returns.description: Whether the Image's internal pixel format is linear (not gamma corrected), when gamma-correct rendering is enabled.

symbols.name: replacePixels
symbols.description: Replace the contents of an Image.

arguments.name: data
arguments.description: The new ImageData to replace the contents with.

arguments.name: slice
arguments.description: Which cubemap face, array index, or volume layer to replace, if applicable.

arguments.name: mipmap
arguments.description: The mimap level to replace, if the Image has mipmaps.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: The x-offset in pixels from the top-left of the image to replace. The given ImageData's width plus this value must not be greater than the pixel width of the Image's specified mipmap level.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: The y-offset in pixels from the top-left of the image to replace. The given ImageData's height plus this value must not be greater than the pixel height of the Image's specified mipmap level.

arguments.name: reloadmipmaps
arguments.description: Whether to generate new mipmaps after replacing the Image's pixels. True by default if the Image was created with automatically generated mipmaps, false by default otherwise.

symbols.name: attachAttribute
symbols.description: Attaches a vertex attribute from a different Mesh onto this Mesh, for use when drawing. This can be used to share vertex attribute data between several different Meshes.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: The name of the vertex attribute to attach.

arguments.name: mesh
arguments.description: The Mesh to get the vertex attribute from.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: The name of the vertex attribute to attach.

arguments.name: mesh
arguments.description: The Mesh to get the vertex attribute from.

arguments.name: step
arguments.description: Whether the attribute will be per-vertex or per-instance when the mesh is drawn.

arguments.name: attachname
arguments.description: The name of the attribute to use in shader code. Defaults to the name of the attribute in the given mesh. Can be used to use a different name for this attribute when rendering.

symbols.name: detachAttribute
symbols.description: Removes a previously attached vertex attribute from this Mesh.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: The name of the attached vertex attribute to detach.

returns.name: success
returns.description: Whether the attribute was successfully detached.

symbols.name: flush
symbols.description:
Immediately sends all modified vertex data in the Mesh to the graphics card.

Normally it isn't necessary to call this method as love.graphics.draw(mesh, ...) will do it automatically if needed, but explicitly using **Mesh:flush** gives more control over when the work happens.

If this method is used, it generally shouldn't be called more than once (at most) between love.graphics.draw(mesh, ...) calls.

symbols.name: getDrawMode
symbols.description: Gets the mode used when drawing the Mesh.

returns.name: mode
returns.description: The mode used when drawing the Mesh.

symbols.name: getDrawRange
symbols.description: Gets the range of vertices used when drawing the Mesh.

returns.name: min
returns.description: The index of the first vertex used when drawing, or the index of the first value in the vertex map used if one is set for this Mesh.

returns.name: max
returns.description: The index of the last vertex used when drawing, or the index of the last value in the vertex map used if one is set for this Mesh.

symbols.name: getTexture
symbols.description: Gets the texture (Image or Canvas) used when drawing the Mesh.

returns.name: texture
returns.description: The Image or Canvas to texture the Mesh with when drawing, or nil if none is set.

symbols.name: getVertex
symbols.description:
Gets the properties of a vertex in the Mesh.

In versions prior to 11.0, color and byte component values were within the range of 0 to 255 instead of 0 to 1.

arguments.name: index
arguments.description: The one-based index of the vertex you want to retrieve the information for.

returns.name: attributecomponent
returns.description: The first component of the first vertex attribute in the specified vertex.
Additional components of all vertex attributes in the specified vertex.

arguments.name: index
arguments.description: The index of the vertex you want to retrieve the information for.

returns.name: x
returns.description: The position of the vertex on the x-axis.

returns.name: y
returns.description: The position of the vertex on the y-axis.

returns.name: u
returns.description: The horizontal component of the texture coordinate.

returns.name: v
returns.description: The vertical component of the texture coordinate.

returns.name: r
returns.description: The red component of the vertex's color.

returns.name: g
returns.description: The green component of the vertex's color.

returns.name: b
returns.description: The blue component of the vertex's color.

returns.name: a
returns.description: The alpha component of the vertex's color.

symbols.name: getVertexAttribute
symbols.description:
Gets the properties of a specific attribute within a vertex in the Mesh.

Meshes without a custom vertex format specified in love.graphics.newMesh have position as their first attribute, texture coordinates as their second attribute, and color as their third attribute.

arguments.name: vertexindex
arguments.description: The index of the the vertex you want to retrieve the attribute for (one-based).

arguments.name: attributeindex
arguments.description: The index of the attribute within the vertex to be retrieved (one-based).

returns.name: value1
returns.description: The value of the first component of the attribute.

returns.name: value2
returns.description: The value of the second component of the attribute.
Any additional vertex attribute components.

symbols.name: getVertexCount
symbols.description: Gets the total number of vertices in the Mesh.

returns.name: count
returns.description: The total number of vertices in the mesh.

symbols.name: getVertexFormat
symbols.description: Gets the vertex format that the Mesh was created with.

returns.name: format
returns.description: The vertex format of the Mesh, which is a table containing tables for each vertex attribute the Mesh was created with, in the form of {attribute, ...}.

symbols.name: getVertexMap
symbols.description:
Gets the vertex map for the Mesh. The vertex map describes the order in which the vertices are used when the Mesh is drawn. The vertices, vertex map, and mesh draw mode work together to determine what exactly is displayed on the screen.

If no vertex map has been set previously via Mesh:setVertexMap, then this function will return nil in LÖVE 0.10.0+, or an empty table in 0.9.2 and older.

returns.name: map
returns.description: A table containing the list of vertex indices used when drawing.

symbols.name: isAttributeEnabled
symbols.description: Gets whether a specific vertex attribute in the Mesh is enabled. Vertex data from disabled attributes is not used when drawing the Mesh.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: The name of the vertex attribute to be checked.

returns.name: enabled
returns.description: Whether the vertex attribute is used when drawing this Mesh.

symbols.name: setAttributeEnabled
symbols.description: Enables or disables a specific vertex attribute in the Mesh. Vertex data from disabled attributes is not used when drawing the Mesh.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: The name of the vertex attribute to enable or disable.

arguments.name: enable
arguments.description: Whether the vertex attribute is used when drawing this Mesh.

symbols.name: setDrawMode
symbols.description: Sets the mode used when drawing the Mesh.

arguments.name: mode
arguments.description: The mode to use when drawing the Mesh.

symbols.name: setDrawRange
symbols.description: Restricts the drawn vertices of the Mesh to a subset of the total.

arguments.name: start
arguments.description: The index of the first vertex to use when drawing, or the index of the first value in the vertex map to use if one is set for this Mesh.

arguments.name: count
arguments.description: The number of vertices to use when drawing, or number of values in the vertex map to use if one is set for this Mesh.

symbols.name: setTexture
symbols.description: Sets the texture (Image or Canvas) used when drawing the Mesh.

arguments.name: texture
arguments.description: The Image or Canvas to texture the Mesh with when drawing.

symbols.name: setVertex
symbols.description:
Sets the properties of a vertex in the Mesh.

In versions prior to 11.0, color and byte component values were within the range of 0 to 255 instead of 0 to 1.

arguments.name: index
arguments.description: The index of the the vertex you want to modify (one-based).

arguments.name: attributecomponent
arguments.description: The first component of the first vertex attribute in the specified vertex.
Additional components of all vertex attributes in the specified vertex.

arguments.name: index
arguments.description: The index of the the vertex you want to modify (one-based).

arguments.name: vertex
arguments.description: A table with vertex information, in the form of {attributecomponent, ...}.

arguments.name: index
arguments.description: The index of the the vertex you want to modify (one-based).

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: The position of the vertex on the x-axis.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: The position of the vertex on the y-axis.

arguments.name: u
arguments.description: The horizontal component of the texture coordinate.

arguments.name: v
arguments.description: The vertical component of the texture coordinate.

arguments.name: r
arguments.description: The red component of the vertex's color.

arguments.name: g
arguments.description: The green component of the vertex's color.

arguments.name: b
arguments.description: The blue component of the vertex's color.

arguments.name: a
arguments.description: The alpha component of the vertex's color.

arguments.name: index
arguments.description: The index of the the vertex you want to modify (one-based).

arguments.name: vertex
arguments.description: A table with vertex information.

symbols.name: setVertexAttribute
symbols.description:
Sets the properties of a specific attribute within a vertex in the Mesh.

Meshes without a custom vertex format specified in love.graphics.newMesh have position as their first attribute, texture coordinates as their second attribute, and color as their third attribute.

arguments.name: vertexindex
arguments.description: The index of the the vertex to be modified (one-based).

arguments.name: attributeindex
arguments.description: The index of the attribute within the vertex to be modified (one-based).

arguments.name: value1
arguments.description: The new value for the first component of the attribute.

arguments.name: value2
arguments.description: The new value for the second component of the attribute.
Any additional vertex attribute components.

symbols.name: setVertexMap
symbols.description:
Sets the vertex map for the Mesh. The vertex map describes the order in which the vertices are used when the Mesh is drawn. The vertices, vertex map, and mesh draw mode work together to determine what exactly is displayed on the screen.

The vertex map allows you to re-order or reuse vertices when drawing without changing the actual vertex parameters or duplicating vertices. It is especially useful when combined with different Mesh Draw Modes.

arguments.name: map
arguments.description: A table containing a list of vertex indices to use when drawing. Values must be in the range of Mesh:getVertexCount().

arguments.name: vi1
arguments.description: The index of the first vertex to use when drawing. Must be in the range of Mesh:getVertexCount().

arguments.name: vi2
arguments.description: The index of the second vertex to use when drawing.

arguments.name: vi3
arguments.description: The index of the third vertex to use when drawing.

arguments.name: data
arguments.description: Array of vertex indices to use when drawing. Values must be in the range of Mesh:getVertexCount()-1

arguments.name: datatype
arguments.description: Datatype of the vertex indices array above.

symbols.name: setVertices
symbols.description: Replaces a range of vertices in the Mesh with new ones. The total number of vertices in a Mesh cannot be changed after it has been created. This is often more efficient than calling Mesh:setVertex in a loop.

arguments.name: vertices
arguments.description: The table filled with vertex information tables for each vertex, in the form of {vertex, ...} where each vertex is a table in the form of {attributecomponent, ...}.

arguments.name: startvertex
arguments.description: The index of the first vertex to replace.

arguments.name: count
arguments.description: Amount of vertices to replace.

arguments.name: data
arguments.description: A Data object to copy from. The contents of the Data must match the layout of this Mesh's vertex format.

arguments.name: startvertex
arguments.description: The index of the first vertex to replace.

arguments.name: vertices
arguments.description: The table filled with vertex information tables for each vertex as follows:

symbols.name: clone
symbols.description: Creates an identical copy of the ParticleSystem in the stopped state.

returns.name: particlesystem
returns.description: The new identical copy of this ParticleSystem.

symbols.name: emit
symbols.description: Emits a burst of particles from the particle emitter.

arguments.name: numparticles
arguments.description: The amount of particles to emit. The number of emitted particles will be truncated if the particle system's max buffer size is reached.

symbols.name: getBufferSize
symbols.description: Gets the maximum number of particles the ParticleSystem can have at once.

returns.name: size
returns.description: The maximum number of particles.

symbols.name: getColors
symbols.description:
Gets the series of colors applied to the particle sprite.

In versions prior to 11.0, color component values were within the range of 0 to 255 instead of 0 to 1.

returns.name: r1
returns.description: First color, red component (0-1).

returns.name: g1
returns.description: First color, green component (0-1).

returns.name: b1
returns.description: First color, blue component (0-1).

returns.name: a1
returns.description: First color, alpha component (0-1).

returns.name: r2
returns.description: Second color, red component (0-1).

returns.name: g2
returns.description: Second color, green component (0-1).

returns.name: b2
returns.description: Second color, blue component (0-1).

returns.name: a2
returns.description: Second color, alpha component (0-1).

returns.name: r8
returns.description: Eighth color, red component (0-1).

returns.name: g8
returns.description: Eighth color, green component (0-1).

returns.name: b8
returns.description: Eighth color, blue component (0-1).

returns.name: a8
returns.description: Eighth color, alpha component (0-1).

symbols.name: getCount
symbols.description: Gets the number of particles that are currently in the system.

returns.name: count
returns.description: The current number of live particles.

symbols.name: getDirection
symbols.description: Gets the direction of the particle emitter (in radians).

returns.name: direction
returns.description: The direction of the emitter (radians).

symbols.name: getEmissionArea
symbols.description: Gets the area-based spawn parameters for the particles.

returns.name: distribution
returns.description: The type of distribution for new particles.

returns.name: dx
returns.description: The maximum spawn distance from the emitter along the x-axis for uniform distribution, or the standard deviation along the x-axis for normal distribution.

returns.name: dy
returns.description: The maximum spawn distance from the emitter along the y-axis for uniform distribution, or the standard deviation along the y-axis for normal distribution.

returns.name: angle
returns.description: The angle in radians of the emission area.

returns.name: directionRelativeToCenter
returns.description: True if newly spawned particles will be oriented relative to the center of the emission area, false otherwise.

symbols.name: getEmissionRate
symbols.description: Gets the amount of particles emitted per second.

returns.name: rate
returns.description: The amount of particles per second.

symbols.name: getEmitterLifetime
symbols.description: Gets how long the particle system will emit particles (if -1 then it emits particles forever).

returns.name: life
returns.description: The lifetime of the emitter (in seconds).

symbols.name: getInsertMode
symbols.description: Gets the mode used when the ParticleSystem adds new particles.

returns.name: mode
returns.description: The mode used when the ParticleSystem adds new particles.

symbols.name: getLinearAcceleration
symbols.description:
Gets the linear acceleration (acceleration along the x and y axes) for particles.

Every particle created will accelerate along the x and y axes between xmin,ymin and xmax,ymax.

returns.name: xmin
returns.description: The minimum acceleration along the x axis.

returns.name: ymin
returns.description: The minimum acceleration along the y axis.

returns.name: xmax
returns.description: The maximum acceleration along the x axis.

returns.name: ymax
returns.description: The maximum acceleration along the y axis.

symbols.name: getLinearDamping
symbols.description: Gets the amount of linear damping (constant deceleration) for particles.

returns.name: min
returns.description: The minimum amount of linear damping applied to particles.

returns.name: max
returns.description: The maximum amount of linear damping applied to particles.

symbols.name: getOffset
symbols.description: Gets the particle image's draw offset.

returns.name: ox
returns.description: The x coordinate of the particle image's draw offset.

returns.name: oy
returns.description: The y coordinate of the particle image's draw offset.

symbols.name: getParticleLifetime
symbols.description: Gets the lifetime of the particles.

returns.name: min
returns.description: The minimum life of the particles (in seconds).

returns.name: max
returns.description: The maximum life of the particles (in seconds).

symbols.name: getPosition
symbols.description: Gets the position of the emitter.

returns.name: x
returns.description: Position along x-axis.

returns.name: y
returns.description: Position along y-axis.

symbols.name: getQuads
symbols.description: Gets the series of Quads used for the particle sprites.

returns.name: quads
returns.description: A table containing the Quads used.

symbols.name: getRadialAcceleration
symbols.description: Gets the radial acceleration (away from the emitter).

returns.name: min
returns.description: The minimum acceleration.

returns.name: max
returns.description: The maximum acceleration.

symbols.name: getRotation
symbols.description: Gets the rotation of the image upon particle creation (in radians).

returns.name: min
returns.description: The minimum initial angle (radians).

returns.name: max
returns.description: The maximum initial angle (radians).

symbols.name: getSizeVariation
symbols.description: Gets the amount of size variation (0 meaning no variation and 1 meaning full variation between start and end).

returns.name: variation
returns.description: The amount of variation (0 meaning no variation and 1 meaning full variation between start and end).

symbols.name: getSizes
symbols.description: Gets the series of sizes by which the sprite is scaled. 1.0 is normal size. The particle system will interpolate between each size evenly over the particle's lifetime.

returns.name: size1
returns.description: The first size.

returns.name: size2
returns.description: The second size.

returns.name: size8
returns.description: The eighth size.

symbols.name: getSpeed
symbols.description: Gets the speed of the particles.

returns.name: min
returns.description: The minimum linear speed of the particles.

returns.name: max
returns.description: The maximum linear speed of the particles.

symbols.name: getSpin
symbols.description: Gets the spin of the sprite.

returns.name: min
returns.description: The minimum spin (radians per second).

returns.name: max
returns.description: The maximum spin (radians per second).

returns.name: variation
returns.description: The degree of variation (0 meaning no variation and 1 meaning full variation between start and end).

symbols.name: getSpinVariation
symbols.description: Gets the amount of spin variation (0 meaning no variation and 1 meaning full variation between start and end).

returns.name: variation
returns.description: The amount of variation (0 meaning no variation and 1 meaning full variation between start and end).

symbols.name: getSpread
symbols.description: Gets the amount of directional spread of the particle emitter (in radians).

returns.name: spread
returns.description: The spread of the emitter (radians).

symbols.name: getTangentialAcceleration
symbols.description: Gets the tangential acceleration (acceleration perpendicular to the particle's direction).

returns.name: max
returns.description: The maximum acceleration.

symbols.name: getTexture

returns.description: The Image or Canvas used for the particles.

returns.description: True if system is stopped, false otherwise.

symbols.description: Moves the position of the emitter. This results in smoother particle spawning behaviour than if ParticleSystem:setPosition is used every frame.

symbols.name: setColors
symbols.description:
Sets a series of colors to apply to the particle sprite. The particle system will interpolate between each color evenly over the particle's lifetime.

Arguments can be passed in groups of four, representing the components of the desired RGBA value, or as tables of RGBA component values, with a default alpha value of 1 if only three values are given. At least one color must be specified. A maximum of eight may be used.

In versions prior to 11.0, color component values were within the range of 0 to 255 instead of 0 to 1.

arguments.name: r1
arguments.description: First color, red component (0-1).

arguments.name: g1
arguments.description: First color, green component (0-1).

arguments.name: b1

arguments.name: a1
arguments.description: First color, alpha component (0-1).
Additional colors.

arguments.name: rgba1
arguments.description: First color, a numerical indexed table with the red, green, blue and alpha values as numbers (0-1). The alpha is optional and defaults to 1 if it is left out.
Additional color, a numerical indexed table with the red, green, blue and alpha values as numbers (0-1). The alpha is optional and defaults to 1 if it is left out.

symbols.description:
Sets a series of sizes by which to scale a particle sprite. 1.0 is normal size. The particle system will interpolate between each size evenly over the particle's lifetime.

At least one size must be specified. A maximum of eight may be used.

arguments.name: size1
arguments.description: The first size.

arguments.name: size2
arguments.description: The second size.

arguments.name: size8
arguments.description: The eighth size.

symbols.name: hasUniform
symbols.description:
Gets whether a uniform / extern variable exists in the Shader.

If a graphics driver's shader compiler determines that a uniform / extern variable doesn't affect the final output of the shader, it may optimize the variable out. This function will return false in that case.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: The name of the uniform variable.

returns.name: hasuniform
returns.description: Whether the uniform exists in the shader and affects its final output.

symbols.name: send
symbols.description:
Sends one or more values to a special (''uniform'') variable inside the shader. Uniform variables have to be marked using the ''uniform'' or ''extern'' keyword, e.g.

uniform float time;  // 'float' is the typical number type used in GLSL shaders.

uniform float varsvec2 light_pos;

uniform vec4 colors[4;

The corresponding send calls would be

shader:send('time', t)

shader:send('vars',a,b)

shader:send('light_pos', {light_x, light_y})

shader:send('colors', {r1, g1, b1, a1},  {r2, g2, b2, a2},  {r3, g3, b3, a3},  {r4, g4, b4, a4})

Uniform / extern variables are read-only in the shader code and remain constant until modified by a Shader:send call. Uniform variables can be accessed in both the Vertex and Pixel components of a shader, as long as the variable is declared in each.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the number to send to the shader.

arguments.name: number
arguments.description: Number to send to store in the uniform variable.
Additional numbers to send if the uniform variable is an array.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the vector to send to the shader.

arguments.name: vector
arguments.description: Numbers to send to the uniform variable as a vector. The number of elements in the table determines the type of the vector (e.g. two numbers -> vec2). At least two and at most four numbers can be used.
Additional vectors to send if the uniform variable is an array. All vectors need to be of the same size (e.g. only vec3's).

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the matrix to send to the shader.

arguments.name: matrix
arguments.description: 2x2, 3x3, or 4x4 matrix to send to the uniform variable. Using table form: {{a,b,c,d}, {e,f,g,h}, ... } or (since version 0.10.2) {a,b,c,d, e,f,g,h, ...}. The order in 0.10.2 is column-major; starting in 11.0 it's row-major instead.
Additional matrices of the same type as ''matrix'' to store in a uniform array.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the Texture to send to the shader.

arguments.name: texture
arguments.description: Texture (Image or Canvas) to send to the uniform variable.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the boolean to send to the shader.

arguments.name: boolean
arguments.description: Boolean to send to store in the uniform variable.
Additional booleans to send if the uniform variable is an array.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the matrix to send to the shader.

arguments.name: matrixlayout
arguments.description: The layout (row- or column-major) of the matrix.

arguments.name: matrix
arguments.description: 2x2, 3x3, or 4x4 matrix to send to the uniform variable. Using table form: {{a,b,c,d}, {e,f,g,h}, ... } or {a,b,c,d, e,f,g,h, ...}.
Additional matrices of the same type as ''matrix'' to store in a uniform array.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the uniform to send to the shader.

arguments.name: data
arguments.description: Data object containing the values to send.

arguments.name: offset
arguments.description: Offset in bytes from the start of the Data object.

arguments.name: size
arguments.description: Size in bytes of the data to send. If nil, as many bytes as the specified uniform uses will be copied.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the uniform matrix to send to the shader.

arguments.name: data
arguments.description: Data object containing the values to send.

arguments.name: matrixlayout
arguments.description: The layout (row- or column-major) of the matrix in memory.

arguments.name: offset
arguments.description: Offset in bytes from the start of the Data object.

arguments.name: size
arguments.description: Size in bytes of the data to send. If nil, as many bytes as the specified uniform uses will be copied.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the uniform matrix to send to the shader.

arguments.name: matrixlayout
arguments.description: The layout (row- or column-major) of the matrix in memory.

arguments.name: data
arguments.description: Data object containing the values to send.

arguments.name: offset
arguments.description: Offset in bytes from the start of the Data object.

arguments.name: size
arguments.description: Size in bytes of the data to send. If nil, as many bytes as the specified uniform uses will be copied.

symbols.name: sendColor
symbols.description:
Sends one or more colors to a special (''extern'' / ''uniform'') vec3 or vec4 variable inside the shader. The color components must be in the range of 1. The colors are gamma-corrected if global gamma-correction is enabled.

Extern variables must be marked using the ''extern'' keyword, e.g.

extern vec4 Color;

The corresponding sendColor call would be

shader:sendColor('Color', {r, g, b, a})

Extern variables can be accessed in both the Vertex and Pixel stages of a shader, as long as the variable is declared in each.

In versions prior to 11.0, color component values were within the range of 0 to 255 instead of 0 to 1.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: The name of the color extern variable to send to in the shader.

arguments.name: color
arguments.description: A table with red, green, blue, and optional alpha color components in the range of 1 to send to the extern as a vector.
Additional colors to send in case the extern is an array. All colors need to be of the same size (e.g. only vec3's).

symbols.name: attachAttribute
symbols.description:
Attaches a per-vertex attribute from a Mesh onto this SpriteBatch, for use when drawing. This can be combined with a Shader to augment a SpriteBatch with per-vertex or additional per-sprite information instead of just having per-sprite colors.

Each sprite in a SpriteBatch has 4 vertices in the following order: top-left, bottom-left, top-right, bottom-right. The index returned by SpriteBatch:add (and used by SpriteBatch:set) can used to determine the first vertex of a specific sprite with the formula 1 + 4 * ( id - 1 ).

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: The name of the vertex attribute to attach.

arguments.name: mesh
arguments.description: The Mesh to get the vertex attribute from.

symbols.name: clear
symbols.description: Removes all sprites from the buffer.

symbols.name: flush
symbols.description:
Immediately sends all new and modified sprite data in the batch to the graphics card.

Normally it isn't necessary to call this method as love.graphics.draw(spritebatch, ...) will do it automatically if needed, but explicitly using SpriteBatch:flush gives more control over when the work happens.

If this method is used, it generally shouldn't be called more than once (at most) between love.graphics.draw(spritebatch, ...) calls.

symbols.name: getDPIScale
symbols.description:
Gets the DPI scale factor of the Texture.

The DPI scale factor represents relative pixel density. A DPI scale factor of 2 means the texture has twice the pixel density in each dimension (4 times as many pixels in the same area) compared to a texture with a DPI scale factor of 1.

For example, a texture with pixel dimensions of 100x100 with a DPI scale factor of 2 will be drawn as if it was 50x50. This is useful with high-dpi /  retina displays to easily allow swapping out higher or lower pixel density Images and Canvases without needing any extra manual scaling logic.

returns.name: dpiscale
returns.description: The DPI scale factor of the Texture.

symbols.description:
Gets the width and height in pixels of the Texture.

Texture:getDimensions gets the dimensions of the texture in units scaled by the texture's DPI scale factor, rather than pixels. Use getDimensions for calculations related to drawing the texture (calculating an origin offset, for example), and getPixelDimensions only when dealing specifically with pixels, for example when using Canvas:newImageData.

symbols.name: isReadable
symbols.description:
Gets whether the Texture can be drawn and sent to a Shader.

Canvases created with stencil and/or depth PixelFormats are not readable by default, unless readable=true is specified in the settings table passed into love.graphics.newCanvas.

Non-readable Canvases can still be rendered to.

returns.name: readable
returns.description: Whether the Texture is readable.

symbols.name: setDepthSampleMode
symbols.description:
Sets the comparison mode used when sampling from a depth texture in a shader. Depth texture comparison modes are advanced low-level functionality typically used with shadow mapping in 3D.

When using a depth texture with a comparison mode set in a shader, it must be declared as a sampler2DShadow and used in a GLSL 3 Shader. The result of accessing the texture in the shader will return a float between 0 and 1, proportional to the number of samples (up to 4 samples will be used if bilinear filtering is enabled) that passed the test set by the comparison operation.

Depth texture comparison can only be used with readable depth-formatted Canvases.

arguments.name: compare
arguments.description: The comparison mode used when sampling from this texture in a shader.

symbols.name: setFilter
symbols.description: Sets the filter mode of the Texture.

arguments.name: min
arguments.description: Filter mode to use when minifying the texture (rendering it at a smaller size on-screen than its size in pixels).

arguments.name: mag
arguments.description: Filter mode to use when magnifying the texture (rendering it at a larger size on-screen than its size in pixels).

arguments.name: anisotropy
arguments.description: Maximum amount of anisotropic filtering to use.

symbols.name: setMipmapFilter
symbols.description:
Sets the mipmap filter mode for a Texture. Prior to 11.0 this method only worked on Images.

Mipmapping is useful when drawing a texture at a reduced scale. It can improve performance and reduce aliasing issues.

In created with the mipmaps flag enabled for the mipmap filter to have any effect. In versions prior to 0.10.0 it's best to call this method directly after creating the image with love.graphics.newImage, to avoid bugs in certain graphics drivers.

Due to hardware restrictions and driver bugs, in versions prior to 0.10.0 images that weren't loaded from a CompressedData must have power-of-two dimensions (64x64, 512x256, etc.) to use mipmaps.

arguments.name: filtermode
arguments.description: The filter mode to use in between mipmap levels. 'nearest' will often give better performance.

arguments.name: sharpness
arguments.description: A positive sharpness value makes the texture use a more detailed mipmap level when drawing, at the expense of performance. A negative value does the reverse.

symbols.name: setWrap
symbols.description:
Sets the wrapping properties of a Texture.

This function sets the way a Texture is repeated when it is drawn with a Quad that is larger than the texture's extent, or when a custom Shader is used which uses texture coordinates outside of [0, 1]. A texture may be clamped or set to repeat in both horizontal and vertical directions.

Clamped textures appear only once (with the edges of the texture stretching to fill the extent of the Quad), whereas repeated ones repeat as many times as there is room in the Quad.

arguments.name: horiz
arguments.description: Horizontal wrapping mode of the texture.

arguments.name: vert
arguments.description: Vertical wrapping mode of the texture.

arguments.name: depth
arguments.description: Wrapping mode for the z-axis of a Volume texture.

symbols.name: mapPixel
symbols.description:
Transform an image by applying a function to every pixel.

This function is a higher-order function. It takes another function as a parameter, and calls it once for each pixel in the ImageData.

The passed function is called with six parameters for each pixel in turn. The parameters are numbers that represent the x and y coordinates of the pixel and its red, green, blue and alpha values. The function should return the new red, green, blue, and alpha values for that pixel.

function pixelFunction(x, y, r, g, b, a)

-- template for defining your own pixel mapping function

-- perform computations giving the new values for r, g, b and a

-- ...

return r, g, b, a

end

In versions prior to 11.0, color component values were within the range of 0 to 255 instead of 0 to 1.

arguments.name: pixelFunction
arguments.description: Function to apply to every pixel.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: The x-axis of the top-left corner of the area within the ImageData to apply the function to.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: The y-axis of the top-left corner of the area within the ImageData to apply the function to.

arguments.name: width
arguments.description: The width of the area within the ImageData to apply the function to.

arguments.name: height
arguments.description: The height of the area within the ImageData to apply the function to.

symbols.name: getGamepadMappingString

Gets the full gamepad mapping string of the Joysticks which have the given GUID, or nil if the GUID isn't recognized as a gamepad.

The mapping string contains binding information used to map the Joystick's buttons an axes to the standard gamepad layout, and can be used later with love.joystick.loadGamepadMappings.

symbols.name: loadGamepadMappings
symbols.description:
Loads a gamepad mappings string or file created with love.joystick.saveGamepadMappings.

It also recognizes any SDL gamecontroller mapping string, such as those created with Steam's Big Picture controller configure interface, or this nice database. If a new mapping is loaded for an already known controller GUID, the later version will overwrite the one currently loaded.

arguments.name: filename
arguments.description: The filename to load the mappings string from.

arguments.name: mappings
arguments.description: The mappings string to load.

Saves the virtual gamepad mappings of all recognized as gamepads and have either been recently used or their gamepad bindings have been modified.

The mappings are stored as a string for use with love.joystick.loadGamepadMappings.

arguments.name: filename

returns.name: mappings
returns.description: The mappings string that was written to the file.

returns.name: mappings
returns.description: The mappings string.

symbols.name: setGamepadMapping
symbols.description:
Binds a virtual gamepad input to a button, axis or hat for all Joysticks of a certain type. For example, if this function is used with a GUID returned by a Dualshock 3 controller in OS X, the binding will affect Joystick:getGamepadAxis and Joystick:isGamepadDown for ''all'' Dualshock 3 controllers used with the game when run in OS X.

LÖVE includes built-in gamepad bindings for many common controllers. This function lets you change the bindings or add new ones for types of Joysticks which aren't recognized as gamepads by default.

The virtual gamepad buttons and axes are designed around the Xbox 360 controller layout.

arguments.name: guid
arguments.description: The OS-dependent GUID for the type of Joystick the binding will affect.

arguments.name: button
arguments.description: The virtual gamepad button to bind.

arguments.name: inputtype
arguments.description: The type of input to bind the virtual gamepad button to.

arguments.name: inputindex
arguments.description: The index of the axis, button, or hat to bind the virtual gamepad button to.

arguments.name: hatdir
arguments.description: The direction of the hat, if the virtual gamepad button will be bound to a hat. nil otherwise.

returns.name: success
returns.description: Whether the virtual gamepad button was successfully bound.

arguments.name: guid
arguments.description: The OS-dependent GUID for the type of Joystick the binding will affect.

arguments.name: axis
arguments.description: The virtual gamepad axis to bind.

arguments.description: The index of the axis, button, or hat to bind the virtual gamepad axis to.

arguments.name: hatdir
arguments.description: The direction of the hat, if the virtual gamepad axis will be bound to a hat. nil otherwise.

returns.name: success
