symbols.name: hasBackgroundMusic
symbols.description:
Gets whether another application on the system is playing music in the background.

Currently this is implemented on iOS and Android, and will always return false on other operating systems. The t.audio.mixwithsystem flag in love.conf can be used to configure whether background audio / music from other apps should play while LÖVE is open.

returns.name: backgroundmusic
returns.description: True if the user is playing music in the background via another app, false otherwise.

symbols.name: openURL
symbols.description: Opens a URL with the user's web or file browser.

arguments.name: url
arguments.description: The URL to open. Must be formatted as a proper URL.

returns.name: success
returns.description: Whether the URL was opened successfully.

symbols.name: setClipboardText

symbols.name: vibrate

arguments.name: seconds
arguments.description: The duration to vibrate for. If called on an iOS device, it will always vibrate for 0.5 seconds due to limitations in the iOS system APIs.

symbols.name: getChannel
symbols.description: Creates or retrieves a named thread channel.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: The name of the channel you want to create or retrieve.

returns.name: channel
returns.description: The Channel object associated with the name.

symbols.name: newChannel
symbols.description:
Create a new unnamed thread channel.

One use for them is to pass new unnamed channels to other threads via Channel:push on a named channel.

returns.name: channel
returns.description: The new Channel object.

symbols.name: newThread
symbols.description: Creates a new Thread from a filename, string or FileData object containing Lua code.

arguments.name: filename

returns.name: thread
returns.description: A new Thread that has yet to be started.

arguments.name: fileData

returns.name: thread
returns.description: A new Thread that has yet to be started.

arguments.name: codestring
arguments.description: A string containing the Lua code to use as the source. It needs to either be at least 1024 characters long, or contain at least one newline.

returns.name: thread
returns.description: A new Thread that has yet to be started.

symbols.name: clear
symbols.description: Clears all the messages in the Channel queue.

symbols.name: demand
symbols.description:
Retrieves the value of a Channel message and removes it from the message queue.

It waits until a message is in the queue then returns the message value.

returns.name: value
returns.description: The contents of the message.

arguments.name: timeout
arguments.description: The maximum amount of time to wait.

returns.name: value
returns.description: The contents of the message or nil if the timeout expired.

symbols.name: getCount
symbols.description: Retrieves the number of messages in the thread Channel queue.

returns.name: count
returns.description: The number of messages in the queue.

symbols.name: hasRead
symbols.description: Gets whether a pushed value has been popped or otherwise removed from the Channel.

arguments.name: id
arguments.description: An id value previously returned by Channel:push.

returns.name: hasread
returns.description: Whether the value represented by the id has been removed from the Channel via Channel:pop, Channel:demand, or Channel:clear.

symbols.name: peek
symbols.description:
Retrieves the value of a Channel message, but leaves it in the queue.

It returns nil if there's no message in the queue.

returns.name: value
returns.description: The contents of the message.

symbols.name: performAtomic
symbols.description:
Executes the specified function atomically with respect to this Channel.

Calling multiple methods in a row on the same Channel is often useful. However if multiple Threads are calling this Channel's methods at the same time, the different calls on each Thread might end up interleaved (e.g. one or more of the second thread's calls may happen in between the first thread's calls.)

This method avoids that issue by making sure the Thread calling the method has exclusive access to the Channel until the specified function has returned.

arguments.name: func
arguments.description: The function to call, the form of function(channel, arg1, arg2, ...) end. The Channel is passed as the first argument to the function when it is called.
Additional arguments that the given function will receive when it is called.

returns.name: ret1
returns.description: The first return value of the given function (if any.)
Any other return values.

symbols.name: pop
symbols.description:
Retrieves the value of a Channel message and removes it from the message queue.

It returns nil if there are no messages in the queue.

returns.name: value
returns.description: The contents of the message.

symbols.name: push
symbols.description:
Send a message to the thread Channel.

See Variant for the list of supported types.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: The contents of the message.

returns.name: id
returns.description: Identifier which can be supplied to Channel:hasRead

symbols.name: supply
symbols.description:
Send a message to the thread Channel and wait for a thread to accept it.

See Variant for the list of supported types.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: The contents of the message.

returns.name: success
returns.description: Whether the message was successfully supplied (always true).

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: The contents of the message.

arguments.name: timeout
arguments.description: The maximum amount of time to wait.

returns.name: success
returns.description: Whether the message was successfully supplied before the timeout expired.

symbols.name: getError
symbols.description: Retrieves the error string from the thread if it produced an error.

returns.name: err
returns.description: The error message, or nil if the Thread has not caused an error.

symbols.name: isRunning
symbols.description:
Returns whether the thread is currently running.

Threads which are not running can be (re)started with Thread:start.

returns.name: value
returns.description: True if the thread is running, false otherwise.

symbols.name: start
symbols.description:
Starts the thread.

Beginning with version 0.9.0, threads can be restarted after they have completed their execution.

arguments.description: A string, number, boolean, LÖVE object, or simple table.

symbols.name: wait
symbols.description:
Wait for a thread to finish.

This call will block until the thread finishes.

symbols.name: getAverageDelta

returns.name: delta

symbols.name: getDelta

symbols.name: getFPS

symbols.name: getTime

Returns the value of a timer with an unspecified starting time.

This function should only be used to calculate differences between points in time, as the starting time of the timer is unknown.

returns.name: time
returns.description: The time in seconds. Given as a decimal, accurate to the microsecond.

symbols.name: sleep

symbols.name: step

Measures the time between two frames.

Calling this changes the return value of love.timer.getDelta.

returns.name: dt
returns.description: The time passed (in seconds).

symbols.name: getPosition

symbols.name: close
symbols.description: Closes the window. It can be reopened with love.window.setMode.

symbols.name: fromPixels
symbols.description:
Converts a number from pixels to density-independent units.

The pixel density inside the window might be greater (or smaller) than the 'size' of the window. For example on a retina screen in Mac OS X with the highdpi window flag enabled, the window may take up the same physical size as an 800x600 window, but the area inside the window uses 1600x1200 pixels. love.window.fromPixels(1600) would return 800 in that case.

This function converts coordinates from pixels to the size users are expecting them to display at onscreen. love.window.toPixels does the opposite. The highdpi window flag must be enabled to use the full pixel density of a Retina screen on Mac OS X and iOS. The flag currently does nothing on Windows and Linux, and on Android it is effectively always enabled.

Most LÖVE functions return values and expect arguments in terms of pixels rather than density-independent units.

arguments.name: pixelvalue
arguments.description: A number in pixels to convert to density-independent units.

returns.name: value
returns.description: The converted number, in density-independent units.

arguments.name: px
arguments.description: The x-axis value of a coordinate in pixels.

arguments.name: py
arguments.description: The y-axis value of a coordinate in pixels.

returns.name: x
returns.description: The converted x-axis value of the coordinate, in density-independent units.

returns.name: y
returns.description: The converted y-axis value of the coordinate, in density-independent units.

symbols.name: getDPIScale
symbols.description:
Gets the DPI scale factor associated with the window.

The pixel density inside the window might be greater (or smaller) than the 'size' of the window. For example on a retina screen in Mac OS X with the highdpi window flag enabled, the window may take up the same physical size as an 800x600 window, but the area inside the window uses 1600x1200 pixels. love.window.getDPIScale() would return 2.0 in that case.

The love.window.fromPixels and love.window.toPixels functions can also be used to convert between units.

The highdpi window flag must be enabled to use the full pixel density of a Retina screen on Mac OS X and iOS. The flag currently does nothing on Windows and Linux, and on Android it is effectively always enabled.

returns.name: scale
returns.description: The pixel scale factor associated with the window.

Gets whether the display is allowed to sleep while the program is running.

Display sleep is disabled by default. Some types of input (e.g. joystick button presses) might not prevent the display from sleeping, if display sleep is allowed.

symbols.description:
Gets whether the Window is currently maximized.

The window can be maximized if it is not fullscreen and is resizable, and either the user has pressed the window's Maximize button or love.window.maximize has been called.

Checks if the game window is visible.

The window is considered visible if it's not minimized and the program isn't hidden.

returns.name: visible

symbols.name: maximize
symbols.description:
Makes the window as large as possible.

This function has no effect if the window isn't resizable, since it essentially programmatically presses the window's 'maximize' button.

symbols.description: Minimizes the window to the system's task bar / dock.

symbols.description:
Causes the window to request the attention of the user if it is not in the foreground.

In Windows the taskbar icon will flash, and in OS X the dock icon will bounce.

Sets whether the display is allowed to sleep while the program is running.

Display sleep is disabled by default. Some types of input (e.g. joystick button presses) might not prevent the display from sleeping, if display sleep is allowed.

arguments.name: enable

The window position is in the coordinate space of the specified display.

symbols.description: Displays a message box dialog above the love window. The message box contains a title, optional text, and buttons.

arguments.name: message

arguments.name: attachtowindow
arguments.description: Whether the message box should be attached to the love window or free-floating.

returns.name: success
returns.description: Whether the message box was successfully displayed.

arguments.name: title

arguments.name: message
arguments.description: The text inside the message box.

arguments.name: buttonlist
arguments.description: A table containing a list of button names to show. The table can also contain the fields enterbutton and escapebutton, which should be the index of the default button to use when the user presses 'enter' or 'escape', respectively.

returns.name: pressedbutton

symbols.name: toPixels
symbols.description:
Converts a number from density-independent units to pixels.

The pixel density inside the window might be greater (or smaller) than the 'size' of the window. For example on a retina screen in Mac OS X with the highdpi window flag enabled, the window may take up the same physical size as an 800x600 window, but the area inside the window uses 1600x1200 pixels. love.window.toPixels(800) would return 1600 in that case.

This is used to convert coordinates from the size users are expecting them to display at onscreen to pixels. love.window.fromPixels does the opposite. The highdpi window flag must be enabled to use the full pixel density of a Retina screen on Mac OS X and iOS. The flag currently does nothing on Windows and Linux, and on Android it is effectively always enabled.

Most LÖVE functions return values and expect arguments in terms of pixels rather than density-independent units.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: A number in density-independent units to convert to pixels.

returns.name: pixelvalue
returns.description: The converted number, in pixels.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: The x-axis value of a coordinate in density-independent units to convert to pixels.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: The y-axis value of a coordinate in density-independent units to convert to pixels.

returns.name: px
returns.description: The converted x-axis value of the coordinate, in pixels.

returns.name: py
returns.description: The converted y-axis value of the coordinate, in pixels.

symbols.name: updateMode
symbols.description:
Sets the display mode and properties of the window, without modifying unspecified properties.

If width or height is 0, updateMode will use the width and height of the desktop.

Changing the display mode may have side effects: for example, canvases will be cleared. Make sure to save the contents of canvases beforehand or re-draw to them afterward if you need to.

arguments.name: width
arguments.description: Window width.

arguments.name: height
arguments.description: Window height.

arguments.name: settings
arguments.description: The settings table with the following optional fields. Any field not filled in will use the current value that would be returned by love.window.getMode.

returns.name: success
returns.description: True if successful, false otherwise.

symbols.name: getVersion
symbols.description: Gets the current running version of LÖVE.

returns.name: major

returns.name: minor

symbols.name: hasDeprecationOutput
symbols.description:
Gets whether LÖVE displays warnings when using deprecated functionality. It is disabled by default in fused mode, and enabled by default otherwise.

When deprecation output is enabled, the first use of a formally deprecated LÖVE API will show a message at the bottom of the screen for a short time, and print the message to the console.

returns.name: enabled

symbols.name: isVersionCompatible
symbols.description: Gets whether the given version is compatible with the current running version of LÖVE.

arguments.name: version
arguments.description: The version to check (for example '11.3' or '0.10.2').

returns.name: compatible
returns.description: Whether the given version is compatible with the current running version of LÖVE.

arguments.name: major
arguments.description: The major version to check (for example 11 for 11.3 or 0 for 0.10.2).

arguments.name: minor
arguments.description: The minor version to check (for example 3 for 11.3 or 10 for 0.10.2).

arguments.name: revision
arguments.description: The revision of version to check (for example 0 for 11.3 or 2 for 0.10.2).

returns.name: compatible
returns.description: Whether the given version is compatible with the current running version of LÖVE.

symbols.name: setDeprecationOutput
symbols.description:
Sets whether LÖVE displays warnings when using deprecated functionality. It is disabled by default in fused mode, and enabled by default otherwise.

When deprecation output is enabled, the first use of a formally deprecated LÖVE API will show a message at the bottom of the screen for a short time, and print the message to the console.

arguments.name: enable
arguments.description: Whether to enable or disable deprecation output.

symbols.name: conf
symbols.description: If a file called conf.lua is present in your game folder (or .love file), it is run before the LÖVE modules are loaded. You can use this file to overwrite the love.conf function, which is later called by the LÖVE 'boot' script. Using the love.conf function, you can set some configuration options, and change things like the default size of the window, which modules are loaded, and other stuff.

arguments.name: t
arguments.description:
The love.conf function takes one argument: a table filled with all the default values which you can overwrite to your liking. If you want to change the default window size, for instance, do:

function love.conf(t)
t.window.width = 1024
t.window.height = 768
end

If you don't need the physics module or joystick module, do the following.

function love.conf(t)
t.modules.joystick = false
t.modules.physics = false
end

Setting unused modules to false is encouraged when you release your game. It reduces startup time slightly (especially if the joystick module is disabled) and reduces memory usage (slightly).

Note that you can't disable love.filesystem; it's mandatory. The same goes for the love module itself. love.graphics needs love.window to be enabled.

symbols.name: directorydropped
symbols.description: Callback function triggered when a directory is dragged and dropped onto the window.

arguments.name: path
arguments.description: The full platform-dependent path to the directory. It can be used as an argument to love.filesystem.mount, in order to gain read access to the directory with love.filesystem.

symbols.name: displayrotated
symbols.description: Called when the device display orientation changed, for example, user rotated their phone 180 degrees.

arguments.name: index
arguments.description: The index of the display that changed orientation.

arguments.name: orientation

symbols.description: Callback function used to draw on the screen every frame.

symbols.name: errorhandler
symbols.description: The error handler, used to display error messages.

arguments.name: msg

returns.name: mainLoop
returns.description: Function which handles one frame, including events and rendering, when called. If this is nil then LÖVE exits immediately.

symbols.name: filedropped

arguments.name: file

arguments.name: axis

arguments.name: isrepeat

arguments.name: key

symbols.name: load
symbols.description: This function is called exactly once at the beginning of the game.

arguments.name: arg
arguments.description: Command-line arguments given to the game.

arguments.name: unfilteredArg
arguments.description: Unfiltered command-line arguments given to the executable (see #Notes).

symbols.name: lowmemory
symbols.description:
Callback function triggered when the system is running out of memory on mobile devices.

Mobile operating systems may forcefully kill the game if it uses too much memory, so any non-critical resource should be removed if possible (by setting all variables referencing the resources to '''nil'''), when this event is triggered. Sounds and images in particular tend to use the most memory.

symbols.name: mousefocus
symbols.description: Callback function triggered when window receives or loses mouse focus.

arguments.name: focus
arguments.description: Whether the window has mouse focus or not.

symbols.name: mousemoved
symbols.description: Callback function triggered when the mouse is moved.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: The mouse position on the x-axis.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: The mouse position on the y-axis.

arguments.name: dx
arguments.description: The amount moved along the x-axis since the last time love.mousemoved was called.

arguments.name: dy
arguments.description: The amount moved along the y-axis since the last time love.mousemoved was called.

arguments.name: istouch
arguments.description: True if the mouse button press originated from a touchscreen touch-press.

symbols.name: mousepressed
symbols.description: Callback function triggered when a mouse button is pressed.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: Mouse x position, in pixels.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: Mouse y position, in pixels.

arguments.name: button
arguments.description: The button index that was pressed. 1 is the primary mouse button, 2 is the secondary mouse button and 3 is the middle button. Further buttons are mouse dependent.

arguments.name: istouch
arguments.description: True if the mouse button press originated from a touchscreen touch-press.

arguments.name: presses
arguments.description: The number of presses in a short time frame and small area, used to simulate double, triple clicks

symbols.name: mousereleased
symbols.description: Callback function triggered when a mouse button is released.

arguments.name: x
arguments.description: Mouse x position, in pixels.

arguments.name: y
arguments.description: Mouse y position, in pixels.

arguments.name: button
arguments.description: The button index that was released. 1 is the primary mouse button, 2 is the secondary mouse button and 3 is the middle button. Further buttons are mouse dependent.

arguments.name: istouch
arguments.description: True if the mouse button release originated from a touchscreen touch-release.

arguments.name: presses
arguments.description: The number of presses in a short time frame and small area, used to simulate double, triple clicks

symbols.name: quit
symbols.description: Callback function triggered when the game is closed.

returns.name: r
returns.description: Abort quitting. If true, do not close the game.

symbols.name: resize
symbols.description: Called when the window is resized, for example if the user resizes the window, or if love.window.setMode is called with an unsupported width or height in fullscreen and the window chooses the closest appropriate size.

arguments.name: w
arguments.description: The new width.

arguments.name: h
arguments.description: The new height.

symbols.name: run
symbols.description: The main function, containing the main loop. A sensible default is used when left out.

returns.name: mainLoop
returns.description: Function which handlers one frame, including events and rendering when called.

symbols.name: textedited
symbols.description:
Called when the candidate text for an IME (Input Method Editor) has changed.

The candidate text is not the final text that the user will eventually choose. Use love.textinput for that.

arguments.name: text
arguments.description: The UTF-8 encoded unicode candidate text.

arguments.name: start
arguments.description: The start cursor of the selected candidate text.

arguments.name: length
arguments.description: The length of the selected candidate text. May be 0.

symbols.name: textinput
symbols.description: Called when text has been entered by the user. For example if shift-2 is pressed on an American keyboard layout, the text '@' will be generated.

arguments.name: text
arguments.description: The UTF-8 encoded unicode text.

symbols.name: threaderror
symbols.description: Callback function triggered when a Thread encounters an error.

arguments.name: thread
arguments.description: The thread which produced the error.

arguments.name: errorstr
arguments.description: The error message.

symbols.name: clone
symbols.description: Creates a new copy of the Data object.

returns.name: clone
returns.description: The new copy.

symbols.name: getFFIPointer
symbols.description:
Gets an FFI pointer to the Data.

This function should be preferred instead of Data:getPointer because the latter uses light userdata which can't store more all possible memory addresses on some new ARM64 architectures, when LuaJIT is used.

returns.name: pointer
returns.description: A raw void* pointer to the Data, or nil if FFI is unavailable.

symbols.name: getPointer
symbols.description: Gets a pointer to the Data. Can be used with libraries such as LuaJIT's FFI.

returns.name: pointer
returns.description: A raw pointer to the Data.

symbols.name: getSize
symbols.description: Gets the Data's size in bytes.

returns.name: size
returns.description: The size of the Data in bytes.

symbols.name: getString
symbols.description: Gets the full Data as a string.

returns.name: data
returns.description: The raw data.

symbols.name: release
symbols.description:
Destroys the object's Lua reference. The object will be completely deleted if it's not referenced by any other LÖVE object or thread.

This method can be used to immediately clean up resources without waiting for Lua's garbage collector.

returns.name: success
returns.description: True if the object was released by this call, false if it had been previously released.

symbols.name: type
symbols.description: Gets the type of the object as a string.

returns.name: type
returns.description: The type as a string.

symbols.name: typeOf
symbols.description: Checks whether an object is of a certain type. If the object has the type with the specified name in its hierarchy, this function will return true.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: The name of the type to check for.

returns.name: b
returns.description: True if the object is of the specified type, false otherwise.

types.name: RecordingDevice
types.description: Represents an audio input device capable of recording sounds.

types.name: Source
types.description:
A Source represents audio you can play back.

You can do interesting things with Sources, like set the volume, pitch, and its position relative to the listener. Please note that positional audio only works for mono (i.e. non-stereo) sources.

The Source controls (play/pause/stop) act according to the following state table.

types.name: ByteData
types.description:
Data object containing arbitrary bytes in an contiguous memory.

There are currently no LÖVE functions provided for manipulating the contents of a ByteData, but Data:getPointer can be used with LuaJIT's FFI to access and write to the contents directly.

types.name: CompressedData
types.description:
Represents byte data compressed using a specific algorithm.

love.data.decompress can be used to de-compress the data (or love.math.decompress in 0.10.2 or earlier).

types.name: DroppedFile
types.description:
Represents a file dropped onto the window.

Note that the DroppedFile type can only be obtained from love.filedropped callback, and can't be constructed manually by the user.

types.name: File
types.description: Represents a file on the filesystem. A function that takes a file path can also take a File.

types.name: FileData
types.description: Data representing the contents of a file.

types.name: GlyphData
types.description: A GlyphData represents a drawable symbol of a font Rasterizer.

types.name: Rasterizer
types.description: A Rasterizer handles font rendering, containing the font data (image or TrueType font) and drawable glyphs.

types.name: Canvas
types.description:
A Canvas is used for off-screen rendering. Think of it as an invisible screen that you can draw to, but that will not be visible until you draw it to the actual visible screen. It is also known as "render to texture".

By drawing things that do not change position often (such as background items) to the Canvas, and then drawing the entire Canvas instead of each item,  you can reduce the number of draw operations performed each frame.

In versions prior to love.graphics.isSupported("canvas") could be used to check for support at runtime.

types.name: Drawable
types.description: Superclass for all things that can be drawn on screen. This is an abstract type that can't be created directly.

types.name: Font
types.description: Defines the shape of characters that can be drawn onto the screen.

types.name: Image
types.description: Drawable image type.

types.name: Mesh
types.description: A 2D polygon mesh used for drawing arbitrary textured shapes.

types.name: ParticleSystem
types.description:
A ParticleSystem can be used to create particle effects like fire or smoke.

The particle system has to be created using update it in the update callback to see any changes in the particles emitted.

The particle system won't create any particles unless you call setParticleLifetime and setEmissionRate.

types.name: Quad
types.description:
A quadrilateral (a polygon with four sides and four corners) with texture coordinate information.

Quads can be used to select part of a texture to draw. In this way, one large texture atlas can be loaded, and then split up into sub-images.

types.name: Shader
types.description:
A Shader is used for advanced hardware-accelerated pixel or vertex manipulation. These effects are written in a language based on GLSL (OpenGL Shading Language) with a few things simplified for easier coding.

Potential uses for shaders include HDR/bloom, motion blur, grayscale/invert/sepia/any kind of color effect, reflection/refraction, distortions, bump mapping, and much more! Here is a collection of basic shaders and good starting point to learn: https://github.com/vrld/moonshine

types.name: SpriteBatch
types.description:
Using a single image, draw any number of identical copies of the image using a single call to love.graphics.draw(). This can be used, for example, to draw repeating copies of a single background image with high performance.

A SpriteBatch can be even more useful when the underlying image is a texture atlas (a single image file containing many independent images); by adding Quads to the batch, different sub-images from within the atlas can be drawn.

types.name: Text
types.description: Drawable text.

types.name: Texture
types.description: Superclass for drawable objects which represent a texture. All Textures can be drawn with Quads. This is an abstract type that can't be created directly.

types.name: Video
types.description: A drawable video.

types.name: CompressedImageData
types.description:
Represents compressed image data designed to stay compressed in RAM.

CompressedImageData encompasses standard compressed texture formats such as  DXT1, DXT5, and BC5 / 3Dc.

You can't draw CompressedImageData directly to the screen. See Image for that.

types.name: ImageData
types.description:
Raw (decoded) image data.

You can't draw ImageData directly to screen. See Image for that.

types.name: Joystick
types.description: Represents a physical joystick.

types.name: BezierCurve
types.description:
A Bézier curve object that can evaluate and render Bézier curves of arbitrary degree.

For more information on Bézier curves check this great article on Wikipedia.

types.name: RandomGenerator
types.description: A random number generation object which has its own random state.

types.name: Transform
types.description:
Object containing a coordinate system transformation.

The love.graphics module has several functions and function variants which accept Transform objects.

types.name: Cursor
types.description: Represents a hardware cursor.

types.name: Body
types.description: Bodies are objects with velocity and position.

types.name: ChainShape
types.description:
A ChainShape consists of multiple line segments. It can be used to create the boundaries of your terrain. The shape does not have volume and can only collide with PolygonShape and CircleShape.

Unlike the PolygonShape, the ChainShape does not have a vertices limit or has to form a convex shape, but self intersections are not supported.

types.name: CircleShape
types.description: Circle extends Shape and adds a radius and a local position.

types.name: Contact
types.description: Contacts are objects created to manage collisions in worlds.

types.name: DistanceJoint
types.description: Keeps two bodies at the same distance.

types.name: EdgeShape
types.description: A EdgeShape is a line segment. They can be used to create the boundaries of your terrain. The shape does not have volume and can only collide with PolygonShape and CircleShape.

types.name: Fixture
types.description: Fixtures attach shapes to bodies.

types.name: FrictionJoint
types.description: A FrictionJoint applies friction to a body.

types.name: GearJoint
types.description: Keeps bodies together in such a way that they act like gears.

types.name: Joint
types.description: Attach multiple bodies together to interact in unique ways.

types.name: MotorJoint
types.description: Controls the relative motion between two Bodies. Position and rotation offsets can be specified, as well as the maximum motor force and torque that will be applied to reach the target offsets.

types.name: MouseJoint
types.description: For controlling objects with the mouse.

types.name: PolygonShape
types.description: A PolygonShape is a convex polygon with up to 8 vertices.

types.name: PrismaticJoint
types.description: Restricts relative motion between Bodies to one shared axis.

types.name: PulleyJoint
types.description: Allows you to simulate bodies connected through pulleys.

types.name: RevoluteJoint
types.description: Allow two Bodies to revolve around a shared point.

types.name: RopeJoint
types.description: The RopeJoint enforces a maximum distance between two points on two bodies. It has no other effect.

types.name: Shape
types.description:
Shapes are solid 2d geometrical objects which handle the mass and collision of a Body in love.physics.

Shapes are attached to a Body via a Fixture. The Shape object is copied when this happens.

The Shape's position is relative to the position of the Body it has been attached to.

types.name: WeldJoint
types.description: A WeldJoint essentially glues two bodies together.

types.name: WheelJoint
types.description: Restricts a point on the second body to a line on the first body.

types.name: World
types.description: A world is an object that contains all bodies and joints.

types.name: Decoder
types.description: An object which can gradually decode a sound file.

types.name: SoundData
types.description:
Contains raw audio samples.

You can not play SoundData back directly. You must wrap a Source object around it.

types.name: Channel
types.description: An object which can be used to send and receive data between different threads.

types.name: Thread
types.description: A Thread is a chunk of code that can run in parallel with other threads. Data can be sent between different threads with Channel objects.

types.name: VideoStream
types.description: An object which decodes, streams, and controls Videos.

types.name: Data
types.description: The superclass of all data.

types.name: Object
types.description: The superclass of all LÖVE types.

enums.name: DistanceModel
enums.description:
The different distance models.

Extended information can be found in the chapter "3.4. Attenuation By Distance" of the OpenAL 1.1 specification.

constants.name: none
constants.description: Sources do not get attenuated.

constants.name: inverse
constants.description: Inverse distance attenuation.

constants.name: inverseclamped
constants.description: Inverse distance attenuation. Gain is clamped. In version 0.9.2 and older this is named '''inverse clamped'''.

constants.name: linear
constants.description: Linear attenuation.

constants.name: linearclamped
constants.description: Linear attenuation. Gain is clamped. In version 0.9.2 and older this is named '''linear clamped'''.

constants.name: exponent
constants.description: Exponential attenuation.

constants.name: exponentclamped
constants.description: Exponential attenuation. Gain is clamped. In version 0.9.2 and older this is named '''exponent clamped'''.

enums.name: EffectType
enums.description: The different types of effects supported by love.audio.setEffect.

constants.name: chorus
constants.description: Plays multiple copies of the sound with slight pitch and time variation. Used to make sounds sound "fuller" or "thicker".

constants.name: compressor
constants.description: Decreases the dynamic range of the sound, making the loud and quiet parts closer in volume, producing a more uniform amplitude throughout time.

constants.name: distortion
constants.description: Alters the sound by amplifying it until it clips, shearing off parts of the signal, leading to a compressed and distorted sound.

constants.name: echo
constants.description: Decaying feedback based effect, on the order of seconds. Also known as delay; causes the sound to repeat at regular intervals at a decreasing volume.

constants.name: equalizer
constants.description: Adjust the frequency components of the sound using a 4-band (low-shelf, two band-pass and a high-shelf) equalizer.

constants.name: flanger
constants.description: Plays two copies of the sound; while varying the phase, or equivalently delaying one of them, by amounts on the order of milliseconds, resulting in phasing sounds.

constants.name: reverb
constants.description: Decaying feedback based effect, on the order of milliseconds. Used to simulate the reflection off of the surroundings.

constants.name: ringmodulator
constants.description: An implementation of amplitude modulation; multiplies the source signal with a simple waveform, to produce either volume changes, or inharmonic overtones.

enums.name: EffectWaveform
enums.description: The different types of waveforms that can be used with the '''ringmodulator''' EffectType.

constants.name: sawtooth
constants.description: A sawtooth wave, also known as a ramp wave. Named for its linear rise, and (near-)instantaneous fall along time.

constants.name: sine
constants.description: A sine wave. Follows a trigonometric sine function.

constants.name: square
constants.description: A square wave. Switches between high and low states (near-)instantaneously.

constants.name: triangle
constants.description: A triangle wave. Follows a linear rise and fall that repeats periodically.

enums.name: FilterType
enums.description: Types of filters for Sources.

constants.name: lowpass
constants.description: Low-pass filter. High frequency sounds are attenuated.

constants.name: highpass
constants.description: High-pass filter. Low frequency sounds are attenuated.

constants.name: bandpass
constants.description: Band-pass filter. Both high and low frequency sounds are attenuated based on the given parameters.

enums.name: SourceType
enums.description:
Types of audio sources.

A good rule of thumb is to use stream for music files and static for all short sound effects. Basically, you want to avoid loading large files into memory at once.

constants.name: static
constants.description: The whole audio is decoded.

constants.name: stream
constants.description: The audio is decoded in chunks when needed.

constants.name: queue
constants.description: The audio must be manually queued by the user.

enums.name: TimeUnit
enums.description: Units that represent time.

constants.name: seconds
constants.description: Regular seconds.

constants.name: samples
constants.description: Audio samples.

enums.name: CompressedDataFormat
enums.description: Compressed data formats.

constants.name: lz4
constants.description: The LZ4 compression format. Compresses and decompresses very quickly, but the compression ratio is not the best. LZ4-HC is used when compression level 9 is specified. Some benchmarks are available here.

constants.name: zlib
constants.description: The zlib format is DEFLATE-compressed data with a small bit of header data. Compresses relatively slowly and decompresses moderately quickly, and has a decent compression ratio.

constants.name: gzip
constants.description: The gzip format is DEFLATE-compressed data with a slightly larger header than zlib. Since it uses DEFLATE it has the same compression characteristics as the zlib format.

constants.name: deflate
constants.description: Raw DEFLATE-compressed data (no header).

enums.name: ContainerType
enums.description: Return type of various data-returning functions.

constants.name: data
constants.description: Return type is ByteData.

constants.name: string
constants.description: Return type is string.

enums.name: EncodeFormat
enums.description: Encoding format used to encode or decode data.

constants.name: base64
constants.description: Encode/decode data as base64 binary-to-text encoding.

constants.name: hex
constants.description: Encode/decode data as hexadecimal string.

enums.name: HashFunction
enums.description: Hash algorithm of love.data.hash.

constants.name: md5
constants.description: MD5 hash algorithm (16 bytes).

constants.name: sha1
constants.description: SHA1 hash algorithm (20 bytes).

constants.name: sha224
constants.description: SHA2 hash algorithm with message digest size of 224 bits (28 bytes).

constants.name: sha256
constants.description: SHA2 hash algorithm with message digest size of 256 bits (32 bytes).

constants.name: sha384
constants.description: SHA2 hash algorithm with message digest size of 384 bits (48 bytes).

constants.name: sha512
constants.description: SHA2 hash algorithm with message digest size of 512 bits (64 bytes).

constants.description: Window focus gained or lost

enums.name: BufferMode
enums.description: Buffer modes for File objects.

constants.name: none
constants.description: No buffering. The result of write and append operations appears immediately.

constants.name: line
constants.description: Line buffering. Write and append operations are buffered until a newline is output or the buffer size limit is reached.

constants.name: full
constants.description: Full buffering. Write and append operations are always buffered until the buffer size limit is reached.

enums.name: FileDecoder
enums.description: How to decode a given FileData.

constants.name: file
constants.description: The data is unencoded.

constants.name: base64
constants.description: The data is base64-encoded.

enums.name: FileMode
enums.description: The different modes you can open a File in.

constants.name: r
constants.description: Open a file for read.

constants.name: w
constants.description: Open a file for write.

constants.name: a
constants.description: Open a file for append.

constants.name: c
constants.description: Do not open a file (represents a closed file.)

enums.name: FileType
enums.description: The type of a file.

constants.name: file
constants.description: Regular file.

constants.name: directory
constants.description: Directory.

constants.name: symlink
constants.description: Symbolic link.

constants.name: other
constants.description: Something completely different like a device.

enums.name: HintingMode
enums.description: True Type hinting mode.

constants.name: normal
constants.description: Default hinting. Should be preferred for typical antialiased fonts.

constants.name: light
constants.description: Results in fuzzier text but can sometimes preserve the original glyph shapes of the text better than normal hinting.

constants.name: mono
constants.description: Results in aliased / unsmoothed text with either full opacity or completely transparent pixels. Should be used when antialiasing is not desired for the font.

constants.name: none
constants.description: Disables hinting for the font. Results in fuzzier text.

enums.name: AlignMode
enums.description: Text alignment.

constants.name: center
constants.description: Align text center.

constants.name: left
constants.description: Align text left.

constants.name: right
constants.description: Align text right.

constants.name: justify
constants.description: Align text both left and right.

enums.name: ArcType
enums.description: Different types of arcs that can be drawn.

constants.name: pie
constants.description: The arc is drawn like a slice of pie, with the arc circle connected to the center at its end-points.

constants.name: open
constants.description: The arc circle's two end-points are unconnected when the arc is drawn as a line. Behaves like the "closed" arc type when the arc is drawn in filled mode.

constants.name: closed
constants.description: The arc circle's two end-points are connected to each other.

enums.name: AreaSpreadDistribution
enums.description: Types of particle area spread distribution.

constants.name: uniform
constants.description: Uniform distribution.

constants.name: normal
constants.description: Normal (gaussian) distribution.

constants.name: ellipse
constants.description: Uniform distribution in an ellipse.

constants.name: borderellipse
constants.description: Distribution in an ellipse with particles spawning at the edges of the ellipse.

constants.name: borderrectangle
constants.description: Distribution in a rectangle with particles spawning at the edges of the rectangle.

constants.name: none
constants.description: No distribution - area spread is disabled.

enums.name: BlendAlphaMode
enums.description: Different ways alpha affects color blending. See BlendMode and the BlendMode Formulas for additional notes.

constants.name: alphamultiply
constants.description: The RGB values of what's drawn are multiplied by the alpha values of those colors during blending. This is the default alpha mode.

constants.name: premultiplied
constants.description: The RGB values of what's drawn are '''not''' multiplied by the alpha values of those colors during blending. For most blend modes to work correctly with this alpha mode, the colors of a drawn object need to have had their RGB values multiplied by their alpha values at some point previously ("premultiplied alpha").

enums.name: BlendMode
enums.description: Different ways to do color blending. See BlendAlphaMode and the BlendMode Formulas for additional notes.

constants.name: alpha
constants.description: Alpha blending (normal). The alpha of what's drawn determines its opacity.

constants.name: replace
constants.description: The colors of what's drawn completely replace what was on the screen, with no additional blending. The BlendAlphaMode specified in love.graphics.setBlendMode still affects what happens.

constants.name: screen
constants.description: 'Screen' blending.

constants.name: add
constants.description: The pixel colors of what's drawn are added to the pixel colors already on the screen. The alpha of the screen is not modified.

constants.name: subtract
constants.description: The pixel colors of what's drawn are subtracted from the pixel colors already on the screen. The alpha of the screen is not modified.

constants.name: multiply
constants.description: The pixel colors of what's drawn are multiplied with the pixel colors already on the screen (darkening them). The alpha of drawn objects is multiplied with the alpha of the screen rather than determining how much the colors on the screen are affected, even when the "alphamultiply" BlendAlphaMode is used.

constants.name: lighten
constants.description: The pixel colors of what's drawn are compared to the existing pixel colors, and the larger of the two values for each color component is used. Only works when the "premultiplied" BlendAlphaMode is used in love.graphics.setBlendMode.

constants.name: darken
constants.description: The pixel colors of what's drawn are compared to the existing pixel colors, and the smaller of the two values for each color component is used. Only works when the "premultiplied" BlendAlphaMode is used in love.graphics.setBlendMode.

constants.name: additive
constants.description: Additive blend mode.

constants.name: subtractive
constants.description: Subtractive blend mode.

constants.name: multiplicative
constants.description: Multiply blend mode.

constants.name: premultiplied
constants.description: Premultiplied alpha blend mode.

enums.name: CompareMode
enums.description: Different types of per-pixel stencil test and depth test comparisons. The pixels of an object will be drawn if the comparison succeeds, for each pixel that the object touches.

constants.name: equal
constants.description:
* stencil tests: the stencil value of the pixel must be equal to the supplied value.
* depth tests: the depth value of the drawn object at that pixel must be equal to the existing depth value of that pixel.

constants.name: notequal
constants.description:
* stencil tests: the stencil value of the pixel must not be equal to the supplied value.
* depth tests: the depth value of the drawn object at that pixel must not be equal to the existing depth value of that pixel.

constants.name: less
constants.description:
* stencil tests: the stencil value of the pixel must be less than the supplied value.
* depth tests: the depth value of the drawn object at that pixel must be less than the existing depth value of that pixel.

constants.name: lequal
constants.description:
* stencil tests: the stencil value of the pixel must be less than or equal to the supplied value.
* depth tests: the depth value of the drawn object at that pixel must be less than or equal to the existing depth value of that pixel.

constants.name: gequal
constants.description:
* stencil tests: the stencil value of the pixel must be greater than or equal to the supplied value.
* depth tests: the depth value of the drawn object at that pixel must be greater than or equal to the existing depth value of that pixel.

constants.name: greater
constants.description:
* stencil tests: the stencil value of the pixel must be greater than the supplied value.
* depth tests: the depth value of the drawn object at that pixel must be greater than the existing depth value of that pixel.

constants.name: never
constants.description: Objects will never be drawn.

constants.name: always
constants.description: Objects will always be drawn. Effectively disables the depth or stencil test.

enums.name: CullMode
enums.description: How Mesh geometry is culled when rendering.

constants.name: back
constants.description: Back-facing triangles in Meshes are culled (not rendered). The vertex order of a triangle determines whether it is back- or front-facing.

constants.name: front
constants.description: Front-facing triangles in Meshes are culled.

constants.name: none
constants.description: Both back- and front-facing triangles in Meshes are rendered.

enums.name: DrawMode
enums.description: Controls whether shapes are drawn as an outline, or filled.

constants.name: fill
constants.description: Draw filled shape.

constants.name: line
constants.description: Draw outlined shape.

enums.name: FilterMode
enums.description: How the image is filtered when scaling.

constants.name: linear
constants.description: Scale image with linear interpolation.

constants.name: nearest
constants.description: Scale image with nearest neighbor interpolation.

enums.name: GraphicsFeature
enums.description: Graphics features that can be checked for with love.graphics.getSupported.

constants.name: clampzero
constants.description: Whether the "clampzero" WrapMode is supported.

constants.name: lighten
constants.description: Whether the "lighten" and "darken" BlendModes are supported.

constants.name: multicanvasformats
constants.description: Whether multiple formats can be used in the same love.graphics.setCanvas call.

constants.name: glsl3
constants.description: Whether GLSL 3 Shaders can be used.

constants.name: instancing
constants.description: Whether mesh instancing is supported.

constants.name: fullnpot
constants.description: Whether textures with non-power-of-two dimensions can use mipmapping and the 'repeat' WrapMode.

constants.name: pixelshaderhighp
constants.description: Whether pixel shaders can use "highp" 32 bit floating point numbers (as opposed to just 16 bit or lower precision).

constants.name: shaderderivatives
constants.description: Whether shaders can use the dFdx, dFdy, and fwidth functions for computing derivatives.

enums.name: GraphicsLimit
enums.description: Types of system-dependent graphics limits checked for using love.graphics.getSystemLimits.

constants.name: pointsize
constants.description: The maximum size of points.

constants.name: texturesize
constants.description: The maximum width or height of Images and Canvases.

constants.name: multicanvas
constants.description: The maximum number of simultaneously active canvases (via love.graphics.setCanvas.)

constants.name: canvasmsaa
constants.description: The maximum number of antialiasing samples for a Canvas.

constants.name: texturelayers
constants.description: The maximum number of layers in an Array texture.

constants.name: volumetexturesize
constants.description: The maximum width, height, or depth of a Volume texture.

constants.name: cubetexturesize
constants.description: The maximum width or height of a Cubemap texture.

constants.name: anisotropy
constants.description: The maximum amount of anisotropic filtering. Texture:setMipmapFilter internally clamps the given anisotropy value to the system's limit.

enums.name: IndexDataType
enums.description: Vertex map datatype for Data variant of Mesh:setVertexMap.

constants.name: uint16
constants.description: The vertex map is array of unsigned word (16-bit).

constants.name: uint32
constants.description: The vertex map is array of unsigned dword (32-bit).

enums.name: LineJoin
enums.description: Line join style.

constants.name: miter
constants.description: The ends of the line segments beveled in an angle so that they join seamlessly.

constants.name: none
constants.description: No cap applied to the ends of the line segments.

constants.name: bevel
constants.description: Flattens the point where line segments join together.

enums.name: LineStyle
enums.description: The styles in which lines are drawn.

constants.name: rough
constants.description: Draw rough lines.

constants.name: smooth
constants.description: Draw smooth lines.

enums.name: MeshDrawMode
enums.description: How a Mesh's vertices are used when drawing.

constants.name: fan
constants.description: The vertices create a "fan" shape with the first vertex acting as the hub point. Can be easily used to draw simple convex polygons.

constants.name: strip
constants.description: The vertices create a series of connected triangles using vertices 1, 2, 3, then 3, 2, 4 (note the order), then 3, 4, 5, and so on.

constants.name: triangles
constants.description: The vertices create unconnected triangles.

constants.name: points
constants.description: The vertices are drawn as unconnected points (see love.graphics.setPointSize.)

enums.name: MipmapMode
enums.description: Controls whether a Canvas has mipmaps, and its behaviour when it does.

constants.name: none
constants.description: The Canvas has no mipmaps.

constants.name: auto
constants.description: The Canvas has mipmaps. love.graphics.setCanvas can be used to render to a specific mipmap level, or Canvas:generateMipmaps can (re-)compute all mipmap levels based on the base level.

constants.name: manual
constants.description: The Canvas has mipmaps, and all mipmap levels will automatically be recomputed when switching away from the Canvas with love.graphics.setCanvas.

enums.name: ParticleInsertMode
enums.description: How newly created particles are added to the ParticleSystem.

constants.name: top
constants.description: Particles are inserted at the top of the ParticleSystem's list of particles.

constants.name: bottom
constants.description: Particles are inserted at the bottom of the ParticleSystem's list of particles.

constants.name: random
constants.description: Particles are inserted at random positions in the ParticleSystem's list of particles.

enums.name: SpriteBatchUsage
enums.description: Usage hints for SpriteBatches and Meshes to optimize data storage and access.

constants.name: dynamic
constants.description: The object's data will change occasionally during its lifetime.

constants.name: static
constants.description: The object will not be modified after initial sprites or vertices are added.

constants.name: stream
constants.description: The object data will always change between draws.

enums.name: StackType
enums.description: Graphics state stack types used with love.graphics.push.

constants.name: transform
constants.description: The transformation stack (love.graphics.translate, love.graphics.rotate, etc.)

constants.name: all
constants.description: All love.graphics state, including transform state.

enums.name: StencilAction
enums.description: How a stencil function modifies the stencil values of pixels it touches.

constants.name: replace
constants.description: The stencil value of a pixel will be replaced by the value specified in love.graphics.stencil, if any object touches the pixel.

constants.name: increment
constants.description: The stencil value of a pixel will be incremented by 1 for each object that touches the pixel. If the stencil value reaches 255 it will stay at 255.

constants.name: decrement
constants.description: The stencil value of a pixel will be decremented by 1 for each object that touches the pixel. If the stencil value reaches 0 it will stay at 0.

constants.name: incrementwrap
constants.description: The stencil value of a pixel will be incremented by 1 for each object that touches the pixel. If a stencil value of 255 is incremented it will be set to 0.

constants.name: decrementwrap
constants.description: The stencil value of a pixel will be decremented by 1 for each object that touches the pixel. If the stencil value of 0 is decremented it will be set to 255.

constants.name: invert
constants.description: The stencil value of a pixel will be bitwise-inverted for each object that touches the pixel. If a stencil value of 0 is inverted it will become 255.

enums.name: TextureType
enums.description: Types of textures (2D, cubemap, etc.)

constants.name: 2d
constants.description: Regular 2D texture with width and height.

constants.name: array
constants.description: Several same-size 2D textures organized into a single object. Similar to a texture atlas / sprite sheet, but avoids sprite bleeding and other issues.

constants.name: cube
constants.description: Cubemap texture with 6 faces. Requires a custom shader (and Shader:send) to use. Sampling from a cube texture in a shader takes a 3D direction vector instead of a texture coordinate.

constants.name: volume
constants.description: 3D texture with width, height, and depth. Requires a custom shader to use. Volume textures can have texture filtering applied along the 3rd axis.

enums.name: VertexAttributeStep
enums.description:
The frequency at which a vertex shader fetches the vertex attribute's data from the Mesh when it's drawn.

Per-instance attributes can be used to render a Mesh many times with different positions, colors, or other attributes via a single love.graphics.drawInstanced call, without using the love_InstanceID vertex shader variable.

constants.name: pervertex
constants.description: The vertex attribute will have a unique value for each vertex in the Mesh.

constants.name: perinstance
constants.description: The vertex attribute will have a unique value for each instance of the Mesh.

enums.name: VertexWinding
enums.description: How Mesh geometry vertices are ordered.

constants.name: cw
constants.description: Clockwise.

constants.name: ccw
constants.description: Counter-clockwise.

enums.name: WrapMode
enums.description: How the image wraps inside a Quad with a larger quad size than image size. This also affects how Meshes with texture coordinates which are outside the range of 1 are drawn, and the color returned by the Texel Shader function when using it to sample from texture coordinates outside of the range of 1.

constants.name: clamp
constants.description: Clamp the texture. Appears only once. The area outside the texture's normal range is colored based on the edge pixels of the texture.

constants.name: repeat
constants.description: Repeat the texture. Fills the whole available extent.

constants.name: mirroredrepeat
constants.description: Repeat the texture, flipping it each time it repeats. May produce better visual results than the repeat mode when the texture doesn't seamlessly tile.

constants.name: clampzero
constants.description: Clamp the texture. Fills the area outside the texture's normal range with transparent black (or opaque black for textures with no alpha channel.)

enums.name: CompressedImageFormat
enums.description:
Compressed image data formats. Here and here are a couple overviews of many of the formats.

Unlike traditional PNG or jpeg, these formats stay compressed in RAM and in the graphics card's VRAM. This is good for saving memory space as well as improving performance, since the graphics card will be able to keep more of the image's pixels in its fast-access cache when drawing it.

constants.name: DXT1
constants.description: The DXT1 format. RGB data at 4 bits per pixel (compared to 32 bits for ImageData and regular Images.) Suitable for fully opaque images on desktop systems.

constants.name: DXT3
constants.description: The DXT3 format. RGBA data at 8 bits per pixel. Smooth variations in opacity do not mix well with this format.

constants.name: DXT5
constants.description: The DXT5 format. RGBA data at 8 bits per pixel. Recommended for images with varying opacity on desktop systems.

constants.name: BC4
constants.description: The BC4 format (also known as 3Dc+ or ATI1.) Stores just the red channel, at 4 bits per pixel.

constants.name: BC4s
constants.description: The signed variant of the BC4 format. Same as above but pixel values in the texture are in the range of 1 instead of 1 in shaders.

constants.name: BC5
constants.description: The BC5 format (also known as 3Dc or ATI2.) Stores red and green channels at 8 bits per pixel.

constants.name: BC5s
constants.description: The signed variant of the BC5 format.

constants.name: BC6h
constants.description: The BC6H format. Stores half-precision floating-point RGB data in the range of 65504 at 8 bits per pixel. Suitable for HDR images on desktop systems.

constants.name: BC6hs
constants.description: The signed variant of the BC6H format. Stores RGB data in the range of +65504.

constants.name: BC7
constants.description: The BC7 format (also known as BPTC.) Stores RGB or RGBA data at 8 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ETC1
constants.description: The ETC1 format. RGB data at 4 bits per pixel. Suitable for fully opaque images on older Android devices.

constants.name: ETC2rgb
constants.description: The RGB variant of the ETC2 format. RGB data at 4 bits per pixel. Suitable for fully opaque images on newer mobile devices.

constants.name: ETC2rgba
constants.description: The RGBA variant of the ETC2 format. RGBA data at 8 bits per pixel. Recommended for images with varying opacity on newer mobile devices.

constants.name: ETC2rgba1
constants.description: The RGBA variant of the ETC2 format where pixels are either fully transparent or fully opaque. RGBA data at 4 bits per pixel.

constants.name: EACr
constants.description: The single-channel variant of the EAC format. Stores just the red channel, at 4 bits per pixel.

constants.name: EACrs
constants.description: The signed single-channel variant of the EAC format. Same as above but pixel values in the texture are in the range of 1 instead of 1 in shaders.

constants.name: EACrg
constants.description: The two-channel variant of the EAC format. Stores red and green channels at 8 bits per pixel.

constants.name: EACrgs
constants.description: The signed two-channel variant of the EAC format.

constants.name: PVR1rgb2
constants.description: The 2 bit per pixel RGB variant of the PVRTC1 format. Stores RGB data at 2 bits per pixel. Textures compressed with PVRTC1 formats must be square and power-of-two sized.

constants.name: PVR1rgb4
constants.description: The 4 bit per pixel RGB variant of the PVRTC1 format. Stores RGB data at 4 bits per pixel.

constants.name: PVR1rgba2
constants.description: The 2 bit per pixel RGBA variant of the PVRTC1 format.

constants.name: PVR1rgba4
constants.description: The 4 bit per pixel RGBA variant of the PVRTC1 format.

constants.name: ASTC4x4
constants.description: The 4x4 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 8 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC5x4
constants.description: The 5x4 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 6.4 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC5x5
constants.description: The 5x5 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 5.12 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC6x5
constants.description: The 6x5 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 4.27 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC6x6
constants.description: The 6x6 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 3.56 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC8x5
constants.description: The 8x5 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 3.2 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC8x6
constants.description: The 8x6 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 2.67 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC8x8
constants.description: The 8x8 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 2 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC10x5
constants.description: The 10x5 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 2.56 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC10x6
constants.description: The 10x6 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 2.13 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC10x8
constants.description: The 10x8 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 1.6 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC10x10
constants.description: The 10x10 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 1.28 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC12x10
constants.description: The 12x10 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 1.07 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC12x12
constants.description: The 12x12 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 0.89 bits per pixel.

enums.name: ImageFormat
enums.description: Encoded image formats.

constants.name: tga
constants.description: Targa image format.

constants.name: png
constants.description: PNG image format.

constants.name: jpg
constants.description: JPG image format.

constants.name: bmp
constants.description: BMP image format.

enums.name: PixelFormat
enums.description: Pixel formats for Textures, ImageData, and CompressedImageData.

constants.name: unknown
constants.description: Indicates unknown pixel format, used internally.

constants.name: normal
constants.description: Alias for rgba8, or srgba8 if gamma-correct rendering is enabled.

constants.name: hdr
constants.description: A format suitable for high dynamic range content - an alias for the rgba16f format, normally.

constants.name: r8
constants.description: Single-channel (red component) format (8 bpp).

constants.name: rg8
constants.description: Two channels (red and green components) with 8 bits per channel (16 bpp).

constants.name: rgba8
constants.description: 8 bits per channel (32 bpp) RGBA. Color channel values range from 0-255 (0-1 in shaders).

constants.name: srgba8
constants.description: gamma-correct version of rgba8.

constants.name: r16
constants.description: Single-channel (red component) format (16 bpp).

constants.name: rg16
constants.description: Two channels (red and green components) with 16 bits per channel (32 bpp).

constants.name: rgba16
constants.description: 16 bits per channel (64 bpp) RGBA. Color channel values range from 0-65535 (0-1 in shaders).

constants.name: r16f
constants.description: Floating point single-channel format (16 bpp). Color values can range from [-65504, +65504].

constants.name: rg16f
constants.description: Floating point two-channel format with 16 bits per channel (32 bpp). Color values can range from [-65504, +65504].

constants.name: rgba16f
constants.description: Floating point RGBA with 16 bits per channel (64 bpp). Color values can range from [-65504, +65504].

constants.name: r32f
constants.description: Floating point single-channel format (32 bpp).

constants.name: rg32f
constants.description: Floating point two-channel format with 32 bits per channel (64 bpp).

constants.name: rgba32f
constants.description: Floating point RGBA with 32 bits per channel (128 bpp).

constants.name: la8
constants.description: Same as rg8, but accessed as (L, L, L, A)

constants.name: rgba4
constants.description: 4 bits per channel (16 bpp) RGBA.

constants.name: rgb5a1
constants.description: RGB with 5 bits each, and a 1-bit alpha channel (16 bpp).

constants.name: rgb565
constants.description: RGB with 5, 6, and 5 bits each, respectively (16 bpp). There is no alpha channel in this format.

constants.name: rgb10a2
constants.description: RGB with 10 bits per channel, and a 2-bit alpha channel (32 bpp).

constants.name: rg11b10f
constants.description: Floating point RGB with 11 bits in the red and green channels, and 10 bits in the blue channel (32 bpp). There is no alpha channel. Color values can range from [0, +65024].

constants.name: stencil8
constants.description: No depth buffer and 8-bit stencil buffer.

constants.name: depth16
constants.description: 16-bit depth buffer and no stencil buffer.

constants.name: depth24
constants.description: 24-bit depth buffer and no stencil buffer.

constants.name: depth32f
constants.description: 32-bit float depth buffer and no stencil buffer.

constants.name: depth24stencil8
constants.description: 24-bit depth buffer and 8-bit stencil buffer.

constants.name: depth32fstencil8
constants.description: 32-bit float depth buffer and 8-bit stencil buffer.

constants.name: DXT1
constants.description: The DXT1 format. RGB data at 4 bits per pixel (compared to 32 bits for ImageData and regular Images.) Suitable for fully opaque images on desktop systems.

constants.name: DXT3
constants.description: The DXT3 format. RGBA data at 8 bits per pixel. Smooth variations in opacity do not mix well with this format.

constants.name: DXT5
constants.description: The DXT5 format. RGBA data at 8 bits per pixel. Recommended for images with varying opacity on desktop systems.

constants.name: BC4
constants.description: The BC4 format (also known as 3Dc+ or ATI1.) Stores just the red channel, at 4 bits per pixel.

constants.name: BC4s
constants.description: The signed variant of the BC4 format. Same as above but pixel values in the texture are in the range of 1 instead of 1 in shaders.

constants.name: BC5
constants.description: The BC5 format (also known as 3Dc or ATI2.) Stores red and green channels at 8 bits per pixel.

constants.name: BC5s
constants.description: The signed variant of the BC5 format.

constants.name: BC6h
constants.description: The BC6H format. Stores half-precision floating-point RGB data in the range of 65504 at 8 bits per pixel. Suitable for HDR images on desktop systems.

constants.name: BC6hs
constants.description: The signed variant of the BC6H format. Stores RGB data in the range of +65504.

constants.name: BC7
constants.description: The BC7 format (also known as BPTC.) Stores RGB or RGBA data at 8 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ETC1
constants.description: The ETC1 format. RGB data at 4 bits per pixel. Suitable for fully opaque images on older Android devices.

constants.name: ETC2rgb
constants.description: The RGB variant of the ETC2 format. RGB data at 4 bits per pixel. Suitable for fully opaque images on newer mobile devices.

constants.name: ETC2rgba
constants.description: The RGBA variant of the ETC2 format. RGBA data at 8 bits per pixel. Recommended for images with varying opacity on newer mobile devices.

constants.name: ETC2rgba1
constants.description: The RGBA variant of the ETC2 format where pixels are either fully transparent or fully opaque. RGBA data at 4 bits per pixel.

constants.name: EACr
constants.description: The single-channel variant of the EAC format. Stores just the red channel, at 4 bits per pixel.

constants.name: EACrs
constants.description: The signed single-channel variant of the EAC format. Same as above but pixel values in the texture are in the range of 1 instead of 1 in shaders.

constants.name: EACrg
constants.description: The two-channel variant of the EAC format. Stores red and green channels at 8 bits per pixel.

constants.name: EACrgs
constants.description: The signed two-channel variant of the EAC format.

constants.name: PVR1rgb2
constants.description: The 2 bit per pixel RGB variant of the PVRTC1 format. Stores RGB data at 2 bits per pixel. Textures compressed with PVRTC1 formats must be square and power-of-two sized.

constants.name: PVR1rgb4
constants.description: The 4 bit per pixel RGB variant of the PVRTC1 format. Stores RGB data at 4 bits per pixel.

constants.name: PVR1rgba2
constants.description: The 2 bit per pixel RGBA variant of the PVRTC1 format.

constants.name: PVR1rgba4
constants.description: The 4 bit per pixel RGBA variant of the PVRTC1 format.

constants.name: ASTC4x4
constants.description: The 4x4 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 8 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC5x4
constants.description: The 5x4 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 6.4 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC5x5
constants.description: The 5x5 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 5.12 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC6x5
constants.description: The 6x5 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 4.27 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC6x6
constants.description: The 6x6 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 3.56 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC8x5
constants.description: The 8x5 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 3.2 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC8x6
constants.description: The 8x6 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 2.67 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC8x8
constants.description: The 8x8 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 2 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC10x5
constants.description: The 10x5 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 2.56 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC10x6
constants.description: The 10x6 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 2.13 bits per pixel.

constants.name: ASTC10x8
constants.description: The 10x8 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 1.6 bits per pixel.

constants.description: The 10x10 pixels per block variant of the ASTC format. RGBA data at 1.28 bits per pixel.

enums.name: Scancode
enums.description:
Keyboard scancodes.

Scancodes are keyboard layout-independent, so the scancode "w" will be generated if the key in the same place as the "w" key on an American QWERTY keyboard is pressed, no matter what the key is labelled or what the user's operating system settings are.

Using scancodes, rather than keycodes, is useful because keyboards with layouts differing from the US/UK layout(s) might have keys that generate 'unknown' keycodes, but the scancodes will still be detected. This however would necessitate having a list for each keyboard layout one would choose to support.

One could use textinput or textedited instead, but those only give back the end result of keys used, i.e. you can't get modifiers on their own from it, only the final symbols that were generated.

enums.name: FullscreenType
enums.description: Types of fullscreen modes.

constants.name: desktop
constants.description: Sometimes known as borderless fullscreen windowed mode. A borderless screen-sized window is created which sits on top of all desktop UI elements. The window is automatically resized to match the dimensions of the desktop, and its size cannot be changed.

constants.name: exclusive
constants.description: Standard exclusive-fullscreen mode. Changes the display mode (actual resolution) of the monitor.

constants.name: normal
constants.description: Standard exclusive-fullscreen mode. Changes the display mode (actual resolution) of the monitor.
